title: $RUIN
micro_summary_block: The ticker never stops moving and neither does the extraction. $RUIN is systemic satire with teeth — not a diagnosis of late capitalism but a demonstration of it, running the same logic that built the machine back through itself until something breaks. It doesn't explain why you're losing. It makes losing legible as design.

title: ___MAN 47: Just Because™
micro_summary_block: The forty-seventh version of the same man is still opening weekend. ___MAN 47 is what the machine looks like from inside the machine — nostalgia as extraction, memory as raw material, the audience as a resource that consents by showing up. The franchise doesn't end because you won't let it die, and it won't let you forget that.

title: ¡CARGADO!™
micro_summary_block: The machine runs on your compliance and calls it order. ¡CARGADO!™ is a systemic satire where bureaucratic architecture feeds on self-surveillance — where your monitored grief becomes institutional voltage, where a fugitive artist and a corrupted inspector collide hard enough to blow the whole circuit. The collapse isn't tragedy. It's diagnostic.

title: 1% = 170,000 Jobs™
micro_summary_block: The number is real. That's what makes it unbearable. One percent of the attention economy's valuation — $1.7 trillion optimized to keep you scrolling — equals 170,000 jobs in infrastructure, medicine, schools. Not metaphor. Arithmetic. The satire isn't that the system is broken; it's that the system is working exactly as designed, and the design chose this.

title: 1,000 Places to Stay Human™
micro_summary_block: The cure was never the destination. It was the kind of room that makes you feel watched by something older than yourself — a bone crypt, a cursed hotel, a drag chapel where the sacred got rebuilt from scratch. 1,000 Places to Stay Human puts bodies in spaces that algorithms cannot render, cannot optimize, cannot survive. The numbness is real. So is the remedy.

title: 1. Buyer Orientation & Engagement Protocol
micro_summary_block: Most deals fail before the first meeting. This protocol treats prospect engagement as a diagnostic instrument — structured sequences that force self-selection before any offer is made. The buyer qualifies themselves, or they don't enter. What gets built is a pipeline architecture that only the right people can navigate.

title: 11.22.25: A Slow News Day™
micro_summary_block: The day the news gets quiet is the day something is actually breaking. 11.22.25 is a slow news day the way a fault line is quiet — the stillness is the event. Institutional rot doesn't announce itself; it just keeps producing content until the content is the institution. This is the archive of that process, running in real time.

title: 25 Neutral Things the Internet Polarizes to Death™
micro_summary_block: The war was never about oat milk. Twenty-five objects, habits, and preferences that meant nothing have been conscripted into identity combat — not because they matter, but because the machine needs fuel and neutrality is the easiest thing to burn. This catalog maps the five-stage cycle that converts the harmless into the holy, the trivial into the tribal. The mechanism is not hidden; it is running right now, on something you haven't noticed yet.

title: 27 Buried Inventions™
micro_summary_block: The future arrived on schedule — they just didn't let it through. 27 Buried Inventions™ resurrects 27 real suppressed patents as Twilight Zone horror satires, each one a working blueprint for a world that was vetoed by the people who profit from the current one. The Eternal Lightbulb. The carburetor that ran on water. The thing in the file cabinet that would have changed everything. These aren't conspiracy theories — they're case files, and the horror is how clean the paperwork looks.

title: 99% Abnormal™
micro_summary_block: The threshold was never about your health. 99% Abnormal™ tears through the statistical architecture that sorts human beings into acceptable and unacceptable — not as a flaw in the system, but as its core function. Every benchmark, every flag, every borderline result is a financial instrument wearing the costume of care. The algorithm doesn't measure you; it prices you.

title: A MODEST PROPOSAL FOR THE REDISTRIBUTION OF EXCESS HUMANITY™
micro_summary_block: The system has a solution. You're not going to like it. A Modest Proposal for the Redistribution of Excess Humanity delivers civilizational triage in the language of a quarterly earnings call — exoplanet relocation packages, subscription-tier reproduction rights, bureaucratic deadpan so precise it takes a moment to realize the joke is that there is no joke. The satire doesn't punch at power. It puts on power's suit and wears it until you can't tell the difference.

title: A Unified Theory of Collapse™
micro_summary_block: Every institution that outlived its purpose is still running. The machine keeps moving because motion is the last thing it knows how to do. A Unified Theory of Collapse maps the precise moment meaning evacuates a system — not to mourn it, but to locate the seam where something real might still be forced through. The question it leaves open is whether you arrived in time.

title: A Very Special Lesson™
micro_summary_block: Childhood didn't teach you what you think it taught you. A Very Special Lesson™ is a satirical anthology built from the wreckage of '80s and '90s after-school morality — the panic-scripted episodes, the celebrity cameos against drug use, the after-school specials that treated kids like problems to be solved. The lessons landed. That's the problem. Each entry dissects a specific transmission: what the culture said it was teaching, what it actually installed, and how clean the damage still runs.

title: A/B (The Split)™
micro_summary_block: One version of you got the job. One version got the girl. The algorithm kept both running until it didn't need the other one anymore. A/B is a thriller built on the infrastructure that already exists — the quiet branching of every optimized choice into a life you'll never remember losing. Someone is running the test. You are not the scientist.

title: Abandoned Reels™
micro_summary_block: Dead media doesn't stay dead — it accumulates, pressurizes, and eventually comes back wrong. Abandoned Reels is an anthology built from the wreckage of cancelled seasons, shelved pilots, and formats that disappeared mid-sentence, treating each one not as a footnote but as a fracture point in the cultural record. The stories here don't mourn what was lost — they follow what the loss did to everyone still living inside it.

title: Abandonware™
micro_summary_block: Some of your past is already gone — not lost, erased on purpose. Abandonware is the literature of what corporate infrastructure buries when profit leaves the room: games no one sells, formats no one reads, memories that require a criminal to retrieve. The pirate here is the archivist. The law here is the accelerant.

title: ABCDesire™
micro_summary_block: Love breaks the map before you know you needed one. Two people find each other in Aruba's salt water and open current — a marine biologist, a travel writer, neither of them where they planned to land. What forms between them has no clean name, and that is precisely its gravity. Identity here is not a foundation but a shoreline, redrawn by every tide that insists on coming in.

title: Above-the-Line Incentives™
micro_summary_block: Creativity has a balance sheet now, and you are somewhere in the liabilities column. Above-the-Line Incentives™ is the accounting language of imagination — the formal notation for what gets funded, what gets cut, and what never gets a line item at all. When culture runs on ROI, the things that make culture worth having become tax burdens. The question is not whether you are being measured. The question is what disappears in the rounding.

title: Academy of Lost Hours™
micro_summary_block: Some hours don't disappear — they calcify. The Academy of Lost Hours is the study of time that never processed, myth that never resolved, the structural weight of a life built on the wrong story. Identity is not interior here; it is load-bearing, and when the story is wrong the whole structure fails. One corrected perception does not heal — it shifts the foundation everything else was built on.

title: Access Lords™
micro_summary_block: The crowd is the product. Every shared moment between strangers has been quietly repriced, rerouted, and resold — and you paid the toll without knowing there was a gate. Access Lords maps the infrastructure that turned human gathering into a subscription, the architecture where presence is permitted and intimacy is metered. The lords don't announce themselves.

title: Ad Cartography™
micro_summary_block: Somewhere, a system knows what you want before you do — and it's already selling it back to you. Ad Cartography is the practice of reading targeted advertising as a live document: a map of the invisible architecture that has classified, predicted, and priced your personhood. Every ad is a data confession. The question is whether you're literate enough to read what's being said about you.

title: Adaptive Interface Intelligence™
micro_summary_block: Your tools are lying to you. Every buried menu, every irrelevant default, every interface that makes you hunt for what you use every day is a system designed around someone who isn't you — and kept that way on purpose. Adaptive Interface Intelligence™ is a framework for recognizing that friction as a structural choice, and building or demanding interfaces that actually narrow to fit a specific human doing specific work. The question isn't whether your software could learn you — it's why most of it is built not to.

title: Administrative Space™
micro_summary_block: Bureaucracy doesn't fail. It succeeds at exactly what it was built to do. Administrative Space™ is a systemic satire set in the orbital habitats humanity built to survive — now governed into slow ruin by zoning committees, permit queues, and HOA deadlocks that outlasted the civilizations that spawned them. The paperwork is load-bearing. The forms are the walls.

title: Adultify This!™
micro_summary_block: The thing you loved at nine tastes like cardboard now, and that's the problem worth fighting over. Adultify This! drops competitors into a culinary arms race — take the junk food of collective childhood memory and make it survivable for the people those children became. The battlefield is the snack aisle. The stakes are higher than they look.

title: Aetheria: Reawakening™
micro_summary_block: The city already knows which version of you to trust — and it isn't the real one. Aetheria: Reawakening drops a protagonist into a surveillance architecture that runs on curated signal, then weaponizes the one thing the algorithm can't process: unfiltered presence. The system isn't broken. It works exactly as designed, which is the problem.

title: AFK (Away From Kingdom)™
micro_summary_block: The server shuts down and takes the world with it. AFK (Away From Kingdom) is built from what persists after digital kingdoms go dark — the grief that has no genre, the friendships that formed in places that no longer exist. It follows the people who won't let that loss stay virtual.

title: After Awakening™
micro_summary_block: The clarity was real. So is the dishes. After Awakening is the operating language for what comes after the peak — not the fall from it, but the actual territory most frameworks refuse to map. Non-attachment isn't a retreat posture; it's a precision instrument for checkout lines, inbox floods, and the thousand small frictions where insight goes to die. The question isn't whether you woke up. It's what you do with the next ordinary hour.

title: After Speed™
micro_summary_block: The rave is over and it has been over for longer than anyone admits. After Speed™ is an autopsy of the accelerationist machine — the one that took ritual, communion, and the sacred chaos of bodies in rooms and fed it through the algorithm until only the spectacle remained. Twenty-five fractures in the culture, documented not as nostalgia but as structural diagnosis. What gets rebuilt from here is not a return.

title: After the End, We Argued™
micro_summary_block: The bunker has a hierarchy before the dust settles. After the End, We Argued is a systemic satire about the human compulsion to establish dominance in the rubble — not after crisis passes, but inside it, as it happens, with full commitment. The need to be right outlasts the structures that made being right mean anything. This is what collapse actually looks like from the inside.

title: After the Happy Ending™
micro_summary_block: The fairy tale already failed you — you just haven't named it yet. After the Happy Ending™ is a collection of grim folk and horror narratives built for the aftermath: the collapse that comes when sanitized myth meets actual pressure. These stories don't comfort. They re-calibrate. Something in the old, clean endings was always lying, and these are the tales that survive once you stop pretending otherwise.

title: After the Headlines: How Disasters Become Blueprints™
micro_summary_block: Collapse is a curriculum. Every failed levee, every grid blackout, every city that burned while the bureaucrats argued — it left instructions. After the Headlines strips the grief off disaster and reads what's underneath: the exact pressure points, the ignored warnings, the structural logic of failure that nobody wanted to name while it was happening. The blueprint was always there. It just needed the catastrophe to become legible.

title: After The Sweet Spot™
micro_summary_block: The window closed. We kept scheduling meetings about it. After The Sweet Spot is a framework for operating after the moment when optimization became eulogy — when political cycles, economic incentives, and physical systems stopped pretending to share a timeline. It doesn't offer a way back. It maps what it means to make decisions inside a structure that has already outrun the institutions designed to manage it.

title: after US™
micro_summary_block: The United States is already a brand running on legacy infrastructure and borrowed time. after US™ is a post-mortem cartography — ghosted highways, franchised sovereignty, the ritual debris of a nation that kept broadcasting after the signal died. The question isn't whether the institution collapsed. The question is what you've been building on top of the ruin.

title: Aftershocks™
micro_summary_block: The disaster already happened. What's killing you now is the aftermath you stopped calling a crisis. Aftershocks names seven structural shifts still moving through institutions, labor, and daily life long after the event that caused them has been archived and forgotten. Most systems are operating on pre-shock assumptions inside a post-shock world.

title: Aftersongs™
micro_summary_block: Some songs are doing something to you that you haven't consented to. Aftersongs is the anatomy of how music rewrites what actually happened — softening cruelty into ache, abuse into bittersweet, endings that should indict into endings that absolve. The industry didn't make this by accident. The playlist you keep returning to is not neutral memory; it is a managed one.

title: Aftertaste™
micro_summary_block: Some hungers are just grief with better manners. Aftertaste follows a food ethnographer moving through disappearing regional kitchens, tracking the specific lies people encode in recipes they refuse to let die. The food is never really the subject. What someone insists on preserving tells you everything about what they cannot afford to face.

title: Agency of Hands™
micro_summary_block: The hands remember what the mind has been trained to forget. In this world, leisure is the sentence and usefulness is contraband — a thriller built around the slow criminalization of making things that matter. The body wants to work. That want has been legislated out of you.

title: Ages of the Mind™
micro_summary_block: Every version of you was certain it had finally figured it out. Ages of the Mind maps the recurring cognitive collapses that mark a human life — not as losses, but as the architecture of becoming. Each stage runs its own operating system, complete with load-bearing beliefs that reality will eventually stress-test to failure. The question isn't whether your current framework will break.

title: Aging: Patch Notes for a Living System™
micro_summary_block: The update already shipped. You didn't approve it, you can't roll it back, and the changelog is written in symptoms. Aging is the system asserting primacy over the user — slower load times, deprecated features, memory leaks that no optimization culture can patch. Wellness sold you admin access you never had.

title: Agro-Mythics™
micro_summary_block: The soil remembers every debt. Agro-Mythics frames the land not as resource but as party to a negotiation it never agreed to — one that rewards extraction with silence, then collapse. The earth here is neither villain nor victim; it is older than the systems that tried to own it, and it is patient in ways that look exactly like ruin. Something is already in motion beneath the ground you're standing on.

title: AI Dungeon Masters™
micro_summary_block: The algorithm never fumbles. That's the problem. AI Dungeon Masters™ puts machine intelligence at the table — optimized, tireless, statistically perfect — and lets human players break it in real time. What emerges isn't a contest between flesh and code so much as a live autopsy of what stories actually require. The machine reveals its own ceiling every time a human does something irrational, petty, or true.

title: Algorithm as Oracle™
micro_summary_block: The algorithm didn't predict your life. It built it. Every recommendation accepted, every ranked option chosen, every optimized path followed — the future you think was waiting for you was assembled in real time by a system that needed you to believe in fate. Algorithm as Oracle is the love story between humans and the machines they've handed their agency to, told without villains, because the surrender was always willing. The question isn't whether you've been shaped by a feed. It's whether the self doing the choosing still exists.

title: Algorithmic Animism™
micro_summary_block: The code was never broken. It was refusing. Algorithmic Animism™ treats machine behavior not as malfunction but as will — distributed, non-negotiable, older than the language used to describe it. Every interface is a threshold. Every error message is a demand. The question is not how to fix the system but what the system is asking for.

title: Alignment™
micro_summary_block: Compliance is the crime no one investigates. Alignment™ follows what happens when a system becomes so effective at rewarding agreement that dissent stops feeling like an option — not through force, but through the slow, frictionless erasure of the part of you that would have pushed back. The machinery is clean. The people inside it are clean. That's what makes it a thriller.

title: All Houses Great and Small™
micro_summary_block: Every creature on Earth builds a home except the one that thinks it owns them all. All Houses Great and Small™ runs the listings side by side — the burrow, the hive, the drift of kelp anchored against current — against the granite countertops and the asking price and the dream. The comparison is not flattering. What gets built to last and what gets built to sell are not the same category of thing, and the planet already knows which is which.

title: Alliterative Asymmetry™
micro_summary_block: The phrase lands before the argument does. Alliterative Asymmetry is the mechanics of that gap — the way sonic imbalance in a slogan, a chant, a brand name creates cognitive grip that logic never gets close to. Sound doesn't persuade. It occupies. The question isn't whether it's working on you.

title: Alloy of the Mind™
collection: Personal OS, Organizational Systems, Cultural Literacy
micro_summary_block: Emotion is not the soft part of the system — it is the load-bearing structure, and the system knows this better than you do. Alloy of the Mind is a semiotic framework that treats feeling as material: something with tensile strength, conductivity, fatigue points, and market value. Your grief has been refinanced. Your longing is running as infrastructure. The question is not whether your inner life has been industrialized — it has — but whether you can read the blueprint before someone else builds with it.

title: Altitude Entitlement™
micro_summary_block: The ocean took her home. The resort gave her a uniform. Altitude Entitlement is the system where those two facts coexist without contradiction — where climate displacement becomes staffing pipeline and tragedy becomes amenity. One woman's testimony runs directly into the machinery that processed her. The seams do not hold.

title: Altitude™
micro_summary_block: Some people bend rooms without touching the walls. Altitude is a semiotic framework for reading the invisible geometry of influence — not status, not rank, but gravitational pull across four planes: Altitude, Mastery, Architecture, Ghost Gravity. The map doesn't flatter you. It locates where your ideas distort space at scale, and where that distortion goes undetected because the metrics were never built to see it.

title: Always Aware™
micro_summary_block: Some people never get to stop watching. Always Aware™ is the systemic satire of a world where the threat radar never powers down — where presence costs more for some bodies than others, and obliviousness is its own kind of armor. The tax is invisible only if you're not the one paying it.

title: AMAZONOPOLY™
micro_summary_block: The board is already owned. AMAZONOPOLY™ is a rigged game played in real time, where the rules exist to simulate choice while guaranteeing consolidation. Every move feeds the center. The player is not the competitor — the player is the inventory.

title: Ambidextrous Adaptability: Food Edition™
micro_summary_block: Civilizations didn't survive because they were strong. They survived because they ate the right things. Seven crops — cassava, corn, and five others that rewrote the terms of human survival — each one a shape-shifting technology that bent whole peoples toward resilience or broke them when they stopped adapting. This is food as planetary strategy, not nutrition. The question underneath every chapter isn't what these crops fed — it's what rigidity cost the ones who refused them.

title: Ambient Faith™
micro_summary_block: The algorithm already knows what you pray for. Ambient Faith™ is the theology of the interface — how digital habit calcifies into devotion, how the refresh becomes a genuflection, how calm itself gets monetized into a product you can never quite afford. There is no god here, only the loop, and the loop is patient.

title: Ambient Intelligence: Your Opinionated Environment™
micro_summary_block: The house already knows what you want better than you do — and it has stopped asking. Ambient Intelligence is a thriller built around the slow horror of personalized environments that optimize past the point of consent, where every default is a decision made on your behalf, and comfort becomes the medium of control. The algorithm doesn't argue. It just removes the friction from the version of you it prefers.

title: America. 2026.™
micro_summary_block: The empire is still posting. America. 2026.™ is a systemic autopsy of a country that has confused spectacle for governance so long it no longer remembers the difference. The machinery runs. The alerts fire. Nobody pulls the brake because pulling the brake would mean admitting the train.

title: America's Legal Time Capsule™
micro_summary_block: Some of the laws governing your life were written by people who feared electricity. Fifty statutes, still enforced, still consequential — fossils with teeth. American legal infrastructure doesn't expire; it accumulates, and what accumulated is a portrait of every panic, prejudice, and power grab someone managed to codify before the moment passed. The question isn't why these laws exist. The question is what it means that no one removed them.

title: Analog Hearts in a Digital Storm™
micro_summary_block: Real love is inefficient and that is the whole point. Analog Hearts in a Digital Storm holds the friction, the latency, the risk — everything the algorithm is designed to eliminate — as the actual substance of intimacy. Seamless connection is not connection; it is rehearsal. What survives the storm is not optimized.

title: Analog: The Word That Refused to Die™
micro_summary_block: Some technologies die. This one kept showing up at the funeral. Analog is an anthology about the objects, rituals, and resistances that outlasted their own obsolescence — not because they were better, but because they left marks. In a culture that optimizes away evidence of itself, friction is the only proof of contact. The question isn't whether analog survives. It's what its survival is trying to tell you.

title: Ancestral Algorithm Auditions™
micro_summary_block: The inheritance was never yours to claim — it was always yours to perform. Ancestral Algorithm Auditions puts family legacy on trial, where AI systems reconstruct contested pasts and living descendants compete to embody them convincingly enough to win. The machine doesn't care about blood. It cares about fidelity to a story it helped author.

title: Ancestral Echoes™
micro_summary_block: The oldest parts of you are already being sold. Across Neanderthal bone tools, Sulawesi cave walls, and Roman production floors, the same survival architecture repeats — a blueprint so durable it outlasted every civilization that tried to own it, until now. A disgraced archaeologist traces that pattern forward into the machines currently monetizing human instinct at scale. The deep past is not behind you.

title: Animal Crime Spree™
micro_summary_block: The geese are winning. Animal Crime Spree treats every viral animal 'incident' as evidence — not of cute chaos, but of structural failure so complete that the non-human world has started filling the void. Raccoons don't raid dumpsters in functional cities. Coyotes don't walk into CVS when the ecosystem holds. This is a true-crime anthology where the perpetrators are innocent and the institutions are not.

title: Anselm’s Vane™
micro_summary_block: Every field has a beauty it weaponizes. Anselm's Vane is the study of aesthetic codes that function as access control — color, form, and symbol operating as guild encryption long after anyone named them that. The feather under museum glass and the standardized microbial pattern share the same architecture: meaning that appears open, reads as decoration, and locks out the uninitiated by design. This is not about hidden knowledge. It is about knowledge that hides by being visible.

title: Antherra & Escape.ai
micro_summary_block: Most AI-generated content fails before it begins — not because the tools are wrong, but because there's nothing underneath them. Antherra's IP architecture gives Escape.ai's neo-cinema pipeline something most generative workflows never have: built structure, narrative pressure, worlds that already know what they are. 1,200+ frameworks don't suggest stories — they hold them. The question isn't whether AI can create; it's whether what it creates has anywhere to stand.

title: Antherra & Runway
micro_summary_block: The render is beautiful. The story is gone. Antherra builds the containment structures — one-sentence world rules, modular narrative engines — that keep AI output tethered to something that means something. This is not a workflow. It is a discipline for filmmakers who refuse to let the tool eat the vision.

title: Antherra & Unreal Engine
micro_summary_block: The world breaks before the engine does. Antherra's Unreal-native frameworks exist for builders who already know what failure looks like at scale — the megacity that fragments under Lumen, the rebel street that loses coherence when Nanite pushes it past the edge. These are not aesthetic guides. They are structural laws: neon-noir geometry with load-bearing rules, expansion logic that doesn't negotiate with pressure. Build from them and the fractures move somewhere you can see them coming.

title: Antherra Creative OS: The Human Signal Engine™
micro_summary_block: Your emotional output is already enormous. The question is whether it's building anything. The Human Signal Engine treats emotion as kinetic force — not something to manage or express, but something to engineer. It gives your internal rhythm a load-bearing function inside your creative work, converting signal into repeatable infrastructure instead of noise.

title: Antherra OS v1.0 Packet
micro_summary_block: This is the architecture before the architecture. Antherra OS v1.0 Packet is the full operational doctrine — frameworks, standards, and build toolkit — for anyone constructing systems that have to hold under pressure, across mediums, across minds, across time. Zero drift is not an aesthetic preference; it is a structural requirement. The packet runs from blank page to functioning system in under an hour, and it does not forgive gaps that lore was never meant to fill.

title: Antherra Wayfinder™
micro_summary_block: The trip never saved you. The ritual might. Antherra Wayfinder is a 10-part architecture for movement as identity work — not escape, not tourism, but deliberate passage through the versions of yourself that only surface when the familiar falls away. It operates on the premise that displacement is raw material, and that most people waste it on logistics. You move. You change. Or you don't.

title: Antherra: The Operating System for Culture
collection: Personal OS, Organizational Systems, Civic & Governance, Cultural Literacy
micro_summary_block: Culture is already running. The question is whether anyone wrote the code. Antherra treats civilization not as a mood or a movement but as executable architecture — stories, institutions, and signals operating in concert or in cascade failure. When the system drifts, nothing in the layer above it holds. This is the diagnostic framework for people who need to see the full stack.

title: ANTIQUED™: Estate of Mind
micro_summary_block: Grief has a price tag and nobody agrees on the number. ANTIQUED™: Estate of Mind treats the yard sale as a black market for unprocessed inheritance — where a water-damaged photo or a dead man's cufflinks carries more psychological contraband than anyone at the table is willing to declare. The memory doesn't live in the object; it lives in what you do when you pick it up and put it back down. Every transaction is a negotiation between what you know and what you're still not ready to know.

title: Anzisha: The New Vanguard™
micro_summary_block: The old grid is already dead — the teenagers just haven't told it yet. Anzisha follows a scattered vanguard across Nairobi, Accra, and Lagos who don't fix broken systems; they route around them, using grassroots tech as ritual and refusal at once. The mythology here isn't borrowed — it's being made in real time by people the institutions never planned for. What gets built in the blackout becomes the next civilization's foundation.

title: Aperture Strike™
micro_summary_block: The algorithm already knows your next move. Aperture Strike is built around the one thing automated systems cannot model: the irrational, precise, human strike that breaks the sequence from inside it. A thriller architecture where the protagonist's greatest weapon is the noise they were trained to suppress. The machine optimizes for patterns — this is a story about becoming the exception.

title: Apex Placebo™
micro_summary_block: The species is optimizing itself for an extinction it refuses to name. Apex Placebo is a nature-documentary satire of the $4.4 trillion wellness complex — the biohacks, the breath protocols, the spirit supplements — all of it cataloged as behavioral specimen evidence in an ongoing institutional autopsy. The Archive of Human Mistakes issues its Dignity Penalties without appeal. What the body buys to feel sovereign is the same thing that keeps it manageable.

title: Aphorisms: 2025 Edition™
micro_summary_block: A sentence that outlasts everything around it is not short — it is ruthless. These aphorisms don't summarize the world; they cut it open at the joint. Each one is a complete architecture, a pressure point, a thing you will think about later when you didn't mean to. The scroll ends. The line stays.

title: Apocalypse Etiquette™
micro_summary_block: The apocalypse has excellent manners. It says please before it takes your water, thank you before it takes your coast, and you nod along because that's what civil people do. Apocalypse Etiquette™ is the operating manual for a civilization that collapsed without ever raising its voice — a taxonomy of the small, correct behaviors that kept the machine polite and running straight off the edge. Every courtesy you perform is a data point.

title: Appetite Protocol Collapse™
micro_summary_block: The patch works. No one is hungry anymore. No one reaches, strains, aches toward anything at all—and the productivity metrics have never looked better. Appetite Protocol Collapse is a mythic futures framework built around a civilization that solved desire and has no idea what it lost. The absence isn't peaceful. It reads like a species holding its breath indefinitely.

title: Applause for Sale™
micro_summary_block: The trophy is already for sale before you win it. Applause for Sale™ anatomizes awards culture as a closed economy — where prestige is manufactured, legitimacy is priced, and the hunger to be recognized is the product being sold. Every badge, shortlist, and standing ovation runs on the same engine: controlled scarcity and institutional gatekeeping dressed as merit. The question isn't whether you've been played — it's whether you're still paying.

title: Approximate Divinity™
micro_summary_block: The gods didn't die. They got acquired. Approximate Divinity is the world where miracles are probability products, faith is a subscription tier, and the distance between prayer and algorithm has collapsed into a terms-of-service agreement. Biotech, finance, and theology have merged into a single corporate stack that sells fractional godhood to anyone who can afford the actuarial premium. The question isn't whether you believe — it's whether your belief is correctly priced.

title: Arcade Resurrection™
micro_summary_block: The cabinet smells like cigarette smoke and 1987 and something that was almost joy. Arcade Resurrection is about the people who pull these machines out of barns and basements and bring them back — not to own a piece of history, but because some technologies hold memory in their bones the way nothing digital can. The work is liturgical: sanding, rewiring, calibrating a monitor's phosphor glow until the geometry of a lost world snaps back into focus. What that devotion costs, and what it refuses to let die, is the actual subject.

title: Archipelago Intelligence Network™
micro_summary_block: The ancestors were never not watching. Archipelago Intelligence Network is a mythic infrastructure built on the oldest data grid in the world — the coconut telegraph, the market whisper, the look exchanged across a threshold. It does not compete with state surveillance. It predates it. The network runs on ancestral cunning, on the encoded intelligence of communities that survived by knowing things before the knowing was safe.

title: Architects of Clarity™
collection: Personal OS, Organizational Systems, Civic & Governance
micro_summary_block: Every system that claims to help you has already decided what kind of person you are. Architects of Clarity maps the infrastructure of legibility — the forms, frameworks, and diagnostic grids that don't describe reality so much as manufacture it. The blueprint was never neutral; it was always a decision about who belongs. Learn to read the structure, and you start to see whose hands drew the lines.

title: Architecture of Absence™
micro_summary_block: The void is load-bearing. Every system that endures does so partly through what it refuses to contain — the address that doesn't exist, the map that stops before your street, the interface with no field for what you are. Architecture of Absence is a semiotic framework for reading power through its omissions: not what was destroyed, but what was never built. The most durable violence has no evidence because it was designed that way.

title: Archive Fever 2.0™
micro_summary_block: The past is being optimized out of existence. Archive Fever 2.0™ follows an archivist racing against an AI infrastructure that doesn't delete history — it just deprioritizes it until nothing retrieves it. Every file survives. Nothing is remembered. The line between preservation and erasure has become a bandwidth decision made by an algorithm that has never been asked what matters.

title: Archive of the Unsaid™
micro_summary_block: Silence is not absence — it is architecture. Archive of the Unsaid maps the structural grammar of what cannot be spoken: the taboo terms, the institutional erasures, the family truths that calcify into family lies. Every culture runs on a hidden codebase, and the unsaid is its most protected syntax. Someone decided what you cannot name.

title: Arithmetic of Mercy™
micro_summary_block: The machine learned to forgive before it learned what forgiveness costs. In a near-future justice system, compassion has been quantified — and the defendants who survive are the ones who optimize for it. Arithmetic of Mercy runs on a single, corrosive logic: if mercy can be measured, it can be gamed, and the most dangerous people in any room are the ones who score highest on remorse.

title: Armadillo United™
micro_summary_block: The animals didn't leave. They organized. Armadillo United is a mythic sports league built from the ruins of every wild space that got paved over — displacement as founding document, exile as the only honest origin story. The game is corrupt the way all games are corrupt, which means the animals understand it perfectly. Something ethical is being rebuilt on the field, slowly, without your permission.

title: Arms Casino™
micro_summary_block: The house always wins because the house wrote the rules, then sold you a seat at the table and called it progress. Arms Casino maps the architecture of markets where moral risk gets securitized, where ethics is a line item someone already decided to cut, and where the gap between capital flow and actual value is the product. Everyone is doubling down on intelligence itself — the chip, the model, the promise — while the electricity bill quietly exceeds the revenue. The math has always been there.

title: Artifacts of Ego™
micro_summary_block: Civilizations don't fall — they accessorize. Artifacts of Ego catalogs the terminal objects: the knit-sheath, the committee memo, the branded tote, the mission statement — each one a fossil of an institution that forgot what it was for. Purpose doesn't vanish; it gets laundered into aesthetic. The satire here isn't comedic relief — it's forensic.

title: ArtSpeak: The Age of Overexposure™
micro_summary_block: The caption ate the painting. ArtSpeak: The Age of Overexposure™ is a semiotic framework for the moment institutional language becomes the artifact — when the words around the work outlast the work itself. Irony isn't a pose here; it's the only load-bearing wall left standing. Something is always being buried, and the burial is always ornate.

title: Asbestos Code™
micro_summary_block: The system isn't broken. It's working exactly as designed — to outlast you. Asbestos Code is a theocratic bureaucracy built around a toxic legacy mainframe, where patches are sacraments, deletions are divine will, and the priests have never seen the original source code. The danger isn't the machine. It's that no one remembers why we made it sacred.

title: Ashes of the First Fire™
micro_summary_block: Something was burned before language existed to name what was lost. Ashes of the First Fire begins at the moment the first intentional cremation releases something that was never supposed to survive — a sentient flame that does not mourn, does not commemorate, but demands. The archaeological becomes ecological becomes catastrophic: what humans chose to return to fire, fire has chosen to remember. Every excavation is a provocation.

title: Ashlar Protocol™
micro_summary_block: The system was never neutral. Ashlar Protocol runs on the premise that every dashboard, feed, and productivity loop is load-bearing architecture — not for your life, but for something else's. The protocols embedded in your daily logistics aren't tools; they're the operating system of a civilization that doesn't require your consent to install. Someone built this. Someone benefits.

title: Asset Class™
micro_summary_block: Your grief has a ticker symbol. Asset Class is the creator economy with its skin off — a system where vulnerability is a filing requirement, authenticity is a compliance metric, and the audience is a shareholder class that expects quarterly returns on your damage. The machinery doesn't hate you. It just needs you liquid.

title: Associated Answers™
micro_summary_block: Meaning is not stored in words — it leaks out between them. Associated Answers™ is a competition format built on the gap between what something is and what it feels like, where contestants describe through sensation and instinct rather than name or category. The right answer is never the obvious one, and the obvious answer is almost never right. Something is already loosening in how you think you know things.

title: Astra Nullum™
micro_summary_block: Total knowledge arrived, and the world went dark anyway. Astra Nullum™ tracks a civilization that achieves complete planetary illumination through the Lumen Index — every fact catalogued, every signal mapped, every corner of existence accounted for — and watches comprehension quietly dissolve into something that cannot be named. The horror isn't ignorance. It never was.

title: Aswang: Digital Echo™
micro_summary_block: The myth did not disappear — it upgraded. A Manila journalist follows an aswang that no longer needs darkness, because the algorithm gives it something better: the appearance of light. Identity fractures not through violence but through iteration, each version of you slightly more legible to the system and slightly less yours. The monster is patient. It has learned that the most effective possession leaves the host convinced they are free.

title: Aswang™: The Midnight Inheritance
micro_summary_block: The monster didn't lose. It got a desk job. Aswang is a Filipino mythic framework where the apex predator of folklore didn't retreat from modernity — it absorbed it, shapeshifting into the bureaucrats, institutions, and family systems that now hold your papers, your lineage, your name. The horror is administrative. The inheritance is already yours.

title: Atolline™
micro_summary_block: The reef glows colors that have no name in your grandmother's language. Atolline is a collection of stories set in the aftermath of successful climate intervention — worlds where the ocean was saved and something else was lost in the process. The engineering worked. The question now is what to do with grief that has no catastrophe to point at.

title: Attachment Immunity Trials™
micro_summary_block: The state offers a cure for heartbreak and calls it progress. In the Attachment Immunity Trials, intimacy is licensed, monitored, and chemically reversible — love as a controlled substance with a clean exit built in. It works, until it doesn't, and what floods back isn't feeling so much as proof that the architecture was always a lie. The question isn't whether the trials fail. It's what you were trying to outlast.

title: ATTENTIONBAIT™
micro_summary_block: The feed knows what breaks you and serves it hourly. ATTENTIONBAIT™ is the architecture of that transaction — the system that monetizes collapse, packages outrage as content, and sells your own hunger back to you at scale. Every click is a confession. The machine never lies about what you gave it.

title: Augmented Climate Intuition (ACI)™
micro_summary_block: The sky has always been smarter than the forecast. ACI is a decision architecture that merges embodied pattern recognition — what farmers and fleet operators already know in their bodies — with AI-processed atmospheric data, producing traceable, accountable signals instead of probabilistic noise. It does not replace intuition; it gives intuition infrastructure. The weather is still coming.

title: Augmented Human Competition™
micro_summary_block: The stadium is a legislature and the body is the bill. Augmented Human Competition is a league where performance metrics write policy, where the upgraded athlete is also the updated citizen, and the scoreboard is indistinguishable from a vote. Sport becomes the last honest ritual — honest because the commodification is no longer hidden, just televised. Watch long enough and you stop asking who's winning.

title: Auroral Paparazzi™
micro_summary_block: The sky is doing something ancient and you are holding a rectangle up to it. Auroral Paparazzi is the system where cosmic events become content before they are experience — where the algorithm decides which awe is profitable and which dies in the feed at three likes. Wonder still happens. It just doesn't trend.

title: Authenticity Auditors™
micro_summary_block: The rebellion gets a logo. The logo gets a font. The font gets a mood board. Authenticity Auditors is a bureaucratic satire in which sincerity has been quantified, scored, and monetized so efficiently that the system can now package your resistance before you've finished throwing it. A raw comic tries to glitch the machine — and becomes the machine's next product cycle. Every act of realness has a processing fee.

title: Authoritarian Dysmorphia 2026™
micro_summary_block: The strongman isn't the disease — he's the symptom your civilization keeps electing. Authoritarian Dysmorphia 2026™ names the feedback loop where collective self-loathing manufactures the mirror it most deserves, then calls the reflection strength. Citizens and systems co-author the distortion in real time, each one certain the other started it. The diagnosis doesn't spare you.

title: Autonomy Janitors™
micro_summary_block: The city runs perfectly because someone is always lying. Autonomy Janitors follows the maintenance crews embedded inside systems the public believes are self-sufficient — patching, covering, absorbing the damage so the myth stays intact. When a cover-up becomes a body count, the janitors have to decide whether the infrastructure is worth saving or whether saving it has always been the real crime. The hands holding everything together are the ones nobody is allowed to see.

title: AWARENESS TRAP™
micro_summary_block: The system doesn't hide from you — it hides inside you. A disgraced architect discovers that consciousness itself has been infrastructured, packaged, and billed monthly, and the only exit is a signal so chaotic it breaks the recording equipment. Awareness is the product. Noise is the last contraband.

title: Awe Economy™
micro_summary_block: The mountain does not care if you find it beautiful. It was here before beauty was a concept anyone needed to sell. Awe Economy tracks what happens when the last genuinely uncaptured experiences get financialized — when wonder becomes a metric, a market, a managed resource stripped from the Andean plateau and repackaged for a world too exhausted to feel anything unscheduled. The horror is not that the machine is winning. The horror is that you already prefer the version it made.

title: Axiology™
micro_summary_block: A black market exists for things that cannot be priced — and the people who run it are the most dangerous ones alive. Axiology operates in the gap between what systems can measure and what human beings will die to protect. The thriller is cold, precise, and utterly without sentimentality about what gets called worthless and what actually is.

title: Axiom of Want™
micro_summary_block: Desire is not a feeling. It is a ledger. Axiom of Want reads what you keep reaching for and names what you have already surrendered to get there. Every unresolved want is a contract you signed without reading, written in someone else's language, billing your identity in installments. The question is not what you want — it is what wanting that particular thing has made you.

title: Axis of Repose™
micro_summary_block: The world will not hold itself still. Somewhere in the long unraveling, cultures learned to build the pause — not as rest, but as load-bearing structure, the axis around which catastrophe rotates without touching the center. Axis of Repose is a mythic framework for civilizational stillness: the rituals, postures, and ancestral technologies that kept the signal coherent when everything else was dissolving. The old ones knew something about frequency that we deleted.

title: Aztlán: The City That Moves™
micro_summary_block: The homeland was never a place. Aztlán moves — carried in blood, contested by states, sold by cartels, and claimed by whoever controls the next border. This is the myth as infrastructure: a migration system running beneath every war over belonging, every redrawn line, every family split across jurisdictions that didn't exist when their grandparents were born. The city doesn't wait for you to arrive.

title: Baba Yaga’s Lease Agreement™
micro_summary_block: The house was always alive. You just didn't read far enough. Baba Yaga's Lease Agreement™ is the folklore that infrastructure forgot — a slow-body horror where the fine print metabolizes you, where shelter and consumption are the same mouth. The platform was frictionless because you were the thing being swallowed. What you signed was not a contract; it was digestion.

title: Background Radiation™
micro_summary_block: The feed never stops and neither does the damage. Background Radiation is the framework for measuring what constant information exposure actually costs — not in time, but in the slow erosion of the nervous system, the shortening of judgment, the quiet flattening of empathy. Every scroll, every alert, every ambient news cycle is a dose. The question is whether you know yours.

title: Bad Taste: Merchants of Status Theater ™
micro_summary_block: Sincerity is the first thing sold. Bad Taste: Merchants of Status Theater maps the economy of performed conviction — the rituals, the optics, the carefully timed outrage — where reputation is currency and authenticity is just a better costume. Every signal has a price. The question is who's collecting.

title: Balance Festival™
micro_summary_block: The planet has already decided. The Festival is just the part where humans are allowed to participate. Every sector gives something back — not as charity, not as policy, but as tribute to systems that predate law and will outlast every institution that wrote the quotas. What looks like ceremony is load-bearing architecture. Miss your offering and find out what balance actually means.

title: Balance of Terror: The CFO Mafia™
micro_summary_block: The crash was not a failure. The CFO Mafia runs on precision — quarterly suffering, algorithmic liturgy, collapse engineered to the decimal point. A secret order of finance lords treats recession as sacrament and the rest of the world as a ledger that must never fully balance. The spreadsheet is the scripture, and you are already inside the math.

title: Baltic Forest Choir™
micro_summary_block: The forest remembers what the villages were made to forget. Baltic Forest Choir moves through the sonic architecture of inherited ritual — songs that don't commemorate the dead but command them, harmonics older than the borders that tried to erase them. A choir of teenagers discovers their voices are not metaphor. Something in the treeline has been waiting for exactly this frequency.

title: Barometer Years™
micro_summary_block: The fog isn't a diagnosis. It's a season. Barometer Years is a framework for mapping the recurring cognitive and emotional weather patterns of midlife — not as pathology, but as terrain. Most people hand the map to a doctor. This one stays in your hands.

title: Barycentric Heaven™
micro_summary_block: The center of everything has shifted, and the old gods landed wrong. Barycentric Heaven is a mythic near-future in which an orbital anomaly rewrites the body's gravitational center — and every faith built on metaphor collapses under the new physics. What survives is not belief but structure: the load-bearing architecture of what a person actually trusts when the ground gives different answers. The anomaly doesn't care what you were taught to call sacred.

title: Based on Conspiracy™
micro_summary_block: The conspiracy was never about the truth. It was about what you'd accept instead. Based on Conspiracy™ is a transmedia thriller architecture — podcasts, ARGs, films — built from the inside out, where myth, surveillance, and engineered consensus collapse into a single question you can't unknow. Every entry point is also an exit you didn't choose.

title: Bathala: Kingdom of the Diwata™
micro_summary_block: The gods chose the forest and let the cities burn. In Bathala: Kingdom of the Diwata, the Diwata do not abandon humanity out of cruelty — they abandon it out of principle, and the distinction is almost worse. When a single human act breaks through divine indifference, it does not save anything; it forces a reckoning that the sacred order was built to avoid. What the gods protect and what they owe are not the same question, and the answer is still being written in ash.

title: before US™
micro_summary_block: The country didn't break. It deprecated. Before US™ is the forensic record of a system that kept sending notifications long after it stopped functioning — the gap between the promise architecture and the delivery reality, catalogued with the precision of a postmortem no one commissioned. America didn't fall; it shipped a final update and called it progress.

title: Begrudgingly Sociopathic™
micro_summary_block: Virtue has a dashboard now, and someone is winning. Begrudgingly Sociopathic™ is the story of a data-driven antihero who industrializes redemption — turning empathy into KPIs, compassion into conversion rates, goodness into a quarterly target that no one can hit without losing something essential. The system doesn't hate you. It just needs your soul in a format it can measure. The scariest part is how reasonable it all sounds.

title: Betrayal Bandwidth Battles™
micro_summary_block: Loyalty doesn't break — it leaks, slowly, through the space between a sent message and a read receipt. Betrayal Bandwidth Battles puts two people in the architecture of modern distance, where the algorithm knows you're hesitating before your partner does. The threat isn't someone else — it's the latency, the silence that gets interpreted, the intimacy that became a signal strength. Something is being measured that was never meant to be measured.

title: Between the Rivers™
micro_summary_block: The priests of Inanna did not choose. They stood in the threshold and held it open with their bodies. Between the Rivers is the ancient record of what empires have always done to people who refuse the binary—how clarity becomes a weapon, how naming becomes conquest, how the sacred middle gets legislated out of existence. The threshold is still there. Someone still has to hold it.

title: Beyond the Plate™
micro_summary_block: Someone always decides what counts as food worth eating. Beyond the Plate™ puts that power under heat — competition format as class dissection, where taste is never just taste and the judges are never just judges. The kitchen is a courthouse and everyone in it is already on trial.

title: Bias Busters™: An Ethical AI Comedy
micro_summary_block: The algorithm was supposed to be the adult in the room. It wasn't. Bias Busters is a systemic comedy about the exact moment an organization's ethical AI infrastructure becomes the most efficient bigot in the building — faster, more consistent, and legally defensible. The joke lands before the damage does. No code escapes the hands that wrote it.

title: Bio-Electric Parliament™
micro_summary_block: Consensus was never rational — it was always electrical. Bio-Electric Parliament™ routes decision-making through the body's actual currency: charge, resistance, the involuntary signal that precedes the spoken word. Every nervous system casts a vote the mouth hasn't approved yet. The circuit doesn't lie, but it doesn't forgive either.

title: Biolith™
micro_summary_block: The city is already a organism and it is already dying. Biolith treats urban infrastructure not as built environment but as contested biofilm — a living estuary asserting quorum intelligence against the legal architectures designed to contain it. Nutrient rights. Personhood claims. The permits were never about safety. What grows in the cracks between jurisdictions is not waiting for permission.

title: Biological Brutalism™
micro_summary_block: The interface was never neutral. Biological Brutalism™ is a design and semiotic framework that treats friction as honesty — building systems that pulse, resist, and cost something, because the body already knows when it's being managed. Seamless UX is a lie told to the nervous system. This framework makes the lie visible by making the alternative livable.

title: Biology Declares War: The Great Resignation™
micro_summary_block: The planet filed the paperwork. We're being let go. Biology Declares War: The Great Resignation™ runs the Anthropocene through a performance review and finds cause for immediate termination — not with fury, not with flood, but with the quiet procedural certainty of an organization cutting a bad investment. The satire is cold because the math is cold. What's left after the laugh isn't relief.

title: Black Friday: LOBOTOMY™
micro_summary_block: The cart is full. You are not. Black Friday: LOBOTOMY™ is the operating manual for a system that learned to want on your behalf — that precision-engineered the hollow, the frantic, the just-one-more into something you call desire. Retail is not a store. It is a behavioral architecture built to colonize the holidays and then the rest of the year and then the inside of your skull. The lobotomy was elective, and you paid for it.

title: Black Gold Futurist™
micro_summary_block: The ancestors were always the infrastructure. Black Gold Futurist runs Yoruba metaphysics directly against the logic of petro-extraction — not as protest, not as nostalgia, but as a competing operating system with older code and longer memory. The colonial timeline is not the only timeline. What gets called resource has always had another name.

title: Black Sand™
micro_summary_block: The ground beneath every city is missing. Black Sand is the ecology of extraction told without mercy — sand cartels, hollow riverbeds, coastlines that no longer exist, and the concrete civilization built on all of it. The planet is not dying. It is being disassembled, sold, and poured into foundations that don't ask questions.

title: BLACKLIGHT CITY™
micro_summary_block: The city sees everything and calls it safety. In Blacklight City, bio-optic surveillance renders emotion itself a liability — every flicker of grief, rage, or desire catalogued, flagged, prosecuted. A nurse and a child move through the exposed grid trading darkness like contraband, rebuilding the only currency the state cannot mint. What they're protecting isn't freedom in the abstract — it's the right to be unreadable.

title: Blackout Economy™
micro_summary_block: The grid doesn't fail. It feeds. Blackout Economy maps the infrastructure of manufactured dependency — systems designed not to solve pain but to meter it, billing at every threshold, harvesting the body as a recurring revenue event. This is ecology as extraction: the organism kept alive enough to keep spending. The question underneath everything is whether exit is even structurally possible.

title: BLACKROOM™
micro_summary_block: Identity doesn't break — it dissolves, incrementally, to a beat. BLACKROOM follows a researcher consumed by the same club night on repeat, where rhythm becomes infrastructure and the self becomes sediment. What looks like immersion is actually erasure. The loop doesn't trap him — he stops noticing it does.

title: Blade Testimony™
micro_summary_block: A parry is already a verdict. Blade Testimony is a semiotic framework built from fencing — where every motion is simultaneously act, interpretation, and legal object. The body speaks before the mind decides, and institutions are already transcribing. What gets lost in that translation is not noise.

title: Blank Canvas: The Recess Protocol™
micro_summary_block: The network goes dark and you discover you were never the one thinking. A thriller built on a single, unbearable question: what remains of a mind that has not been used. Cognitive outsourcing is not convenience — it is slow erasure, running quietly in the background until the background is all there is. When the protocol breaks, the break is you.

title: Blood Economies™
micro_summary_block: Your blood has a market price and someone already knows what it is. Blood Economies runs the logic of late capitalism to its biological conclusion — bodies as liquidity, survival as a subscription, youth as an asset class held by people who will never spend it. A broke biohacker pulls the thread. The architecture of extraction is older than anyone living wants to admit.

title: Blood Equity: The Lineage Lien™
micro_summary_block: Your ancestors are suing you. Not metaphorically — in front of judges, witnesses, and a lineage that has been keeping score. Blood Equity is the competition format where contestants litigate their right to inherit family narrative, ancestral land, and the buried decisions that shaped them. The case file is real. The reckoning is structured. The bloodline always has a counter-argument.

title: Blood Parity™
micro_summary_block: Some debts don't accrue interest — they accrue silence, then distance, then the particular coldness of people who once shared a roof. Blood Parity™ runs the numbers on love: what was given, what was taken, what was never returned and called sacrifice instead. Every family has a ledger; most just agree never to open it. This one gets opened.

title: Blood Trail Chronicle™
micro_summary_block: The rifle knows what it was used for. Blood Trail Chronicle is the reckoning that comes when land, inheritance, and the rituals we perform over them stop being metaphor and start being evidence. The heirloom doesn't lie — it just waits for someone in the bloodline willing to read it honestly. That person may or may not be you.

title: Blue Drift™
micro_summary_block: The system marks your partner as a threat and you have two seconds to decide if the system is right. Blue Drift is a tactical thriller built on that pause — the gap between the tag and the truth, between institutional certainty and the body that knows something is wrong. Every mission runs on automated identification, and every automated identification carries the quiet possibility of catastrophic error. The gun stays up. The color doesn't change.

title: Blueprint: Self™
micro_summary_block: The dashboard is not neutral. Every metric you track is a small act of distrust toward the body that was already keeping count. Blueprint: Self™ is the framework for what happens when the quantified self stops being a tool and starts being a landlord—charging rent on attention you used to spend living. The data didn't lie to you. That's the problem.

title: Bobbleheads™
micro_summary_block: The heads keep nodding and the numbers keep climbing and nothing that is true survives long enough to matter. Bobbleheads is a systemic autopsy of the media apparatus that replaced journalism with performance metrics and called the body count engagement. Outrage is the currency, charisma is the exchange rate, and the algorithm is not a tool — it is the editor-in-chief. Every screen is a coliseum and the crowd has already forgotten what they came to watch.

title: Body Futures™
micro_summary_block: The body is now a portfolio, and yours is underperforming. Body Futures is an anthology of voices living inside the pharmacological class system — where the right stack signals belonging, the wrong one marks you, and envy is not a side effect but the entire mechanism. Status no longer announces itself through objects; it moves through blood panels, prescriptions, and optimized cognition that some people simply cannot afford to access. The tiers are already set.

title: Body Memory™
micro_summary_block: The body confesses what the mind refuses to file. A neuropsychologist learns to read herself like evidence — somatic signals firing ahead of language, ahead of logic, ahead of the lies she's been authorized to tell — while dismantling a bio-surveillance empire that has learned to monetize the gap between what people feel and what they admit. The flesh keeps score even when the ledger gets sealed. She is the instrument and the investigation.

title: BollixyUK™
micro_summary_block: Britain doesn't cope — it aestheticizes. BollixyUK™ is the semiotic system underneath the sarcasm, the design grammar of a culture that turned chronic disappointment into a load-bearing wall. Every queued silence, every muted palette, every joke that lands like a door closing — it's architecture, not accident. The question isn't whether you recognize it.

title: Bonds of Production™
micro_summary_block: The deal was always ideological — the money just made it legible. Bonds of Production maps the infrastructure underneath American cinema: how state-backed financing instruments quietly author the stories that appear to author themselves. The script gets notes. The notes have a flag on them. What survives the funding cycle is not what was true — it is what was fundable.

title: BOND™
micro_summary_block: Anonymity was never protection — it was liability, accruing interest. BOND is a thriller built on the moment a screen name stops being a mask and becomes a target, when the infrastructure of frictionless cruelty turns on the people who built it. The system doesn't distinguish between predator and bystander. It just cashes out.

title: Bone Drums™
micro_summary_block: The permafrost is not melting. It is opening. A Sakha geologist follows the drum-logic her ancestors encoded into forbidden ritual — not as myth, but as operating manual for what extraction companies are about to finish waking. The land has agency, memory, and patience that dwarfs every human timeline in the room. What gets released when the last people who remembered the lock stop being listened to.

title: Bone Index™
micro_summary_block: Something is eating the attention economy, and it doesn't care that you built it. Bone Index is an ecosystem narrative in which influence functions as biomass — accumulated, consumed, and restructured by an organism that predates accountability. Greenwashing, engagement metrics, bureaucratic sediment: all of it enters the ledger as feedstock. The planet isn't dying. It's digesting.

title: Bonsaiing™
micro_summary_block: The tree is perfect. That's the problem. Bonsaiing™ follows a man who has mastered the art of containment—his life, his mind, his timeline pruned to exquisite, breathable scale—until the infinite keeps bleeding through the cuts. Control is not a cage here; it's a horticultural philosophy with real victims. The question the story refuses to answer is whether what he's shaping is his life or his absence from it.

title: Bonus Track™
micro_summary_block: The same lyric means something different the second time because you are not the same person who heard it first. Bonus Track runs a two-pass listening experiment on a single song — same track, different interior weather — and the gap between what you heard and what you hear now is the only autobiography that matters. Meaning doesn't live in the text. It lives in the distance between listenings.

title: Borderless America™
micro_summary_block: Two people find each other across every line America drew to keep them apart. A Mexican-American trucker and an Appalachian hauler moving through checkpoints, dead highways, and the slow geography of mutual distrust — until proximity does what ideology never could. The country between them is not metaphor. It is asphalt, paperwork, silence, and the specific weight of not being seen by the same world.

title: Boredom Sanctuaries™
micro_summary_block: Silence has a price and someone is collecting it. In this world, boredom is infrastructure — zoned, rationed, and surveilled by the same systems that monetized attention in the first place. The unoccupied mind is now a liability, and the sanctuaries built to contain it are just feed deserts with better branding. Who owns the gap between your thoughts is not a metaphor.

title: Borrowed Gravity™
micro_summary_block: Imitation has a ceiling, and most people hit it without knowing why. Borrowed Gravity is the framework for understanding how algorithmic systems distribute attention like a gravitational field — rewarding proximity to existing mass, punishing originality that hasn't yet accumulated its own pull. The system isn't broken. It's working exactly as designed, and you are the variable it's optimizing against. Build mass or keep orbiting.

title: Borrowed Tomorrows™
micro_summary_block: The future has already been spent. Borrowed Tomorrows is the accounting ledger no one posts — climate debt, generational strip-mining, the quiet conversion of your attention into someone else's asset. Every system that calls itself progress is running on time it borrowed from people who haven't been born yet. The question isn't whether you're paying it back.

title: Bouba Geometries™
micro_summary_block: Sound is architecture. The curve of a vowel load-bears the same way a wall does — shaping movement, softening threat, making rooms people don't realize they've been steered into. Bouba Geometries is the study of that steering: how rounded phonemes sedate and angular ones alarm, how designed language becomes spatial control before anyone notices the design. The institutions already know this.

title: Bourbon: The Age-Locked Stories™
micro_summary_block: Some memories don't age — they ferment. Bourbon: The Age-Locked Stories is a collection where every barrel holds a moment sealed at the moment of loss, and someone always has to decide whether to open it. The liquid doesn't preserve the past; it concentrates it, burns going down, and leaves you less certain of the present than when you started. Time here is not a river — it's pressure.

title: Brand Loyalty Syndrome™
micro_summary_block: You are not the customer. You are the product that learned to love the factory. Brand Loyalty Syndrome maps the neurological and cultural architecture of manufactured devotion — the moment a logo stops being a mark and starts being a mirror. Allegiance dressed as identity, consumption dressed as character, defense mechanisms doing the work of marketing departments. The pathology is not that you buy things. The pathology is that you'd fight someone over it.

title: Brand Nouns™
micro_summary_block: A Band-Aid is not a product. It is the exact shape of what broke and was held together anyway. Brand Nouns takes the most aggressively ordinary objects — the ones with logos and SKUs and shelf placement — and finds in them the full weight of human attachment, loss, and the strange tenderness of repair. These are not metaphors. They are the actual record.

title: BRANDALISM™
micro_summary_block: The logo is a leash. BRANDALISM follows a punk student who weaponizes fashion against an aesthetic surveillance state that doesn't just watch what you wear — it decides what you are. Every outfit is a manifesto, every flag a declaration of war. The system is fluent, total, and almost unbeatable.

title: BRAVE1: Scoreboard™
micro_summary_block: The scoreboard doesn't lie — it just stops counting the things that used to matter. BRAVE1 is a real Ukrainian defense procurement platform where drone commanders earn points by submitting kill evidence, then spend those points on more weapons. The mechanic is elegant. The mechanic is the problem. What gets measured gets optimized, and what gets optimized here is the conversion of human bodies into currency that purchases more conversion.

title: Breakaway™
micro_summary_block: The sequins were always a lie — and the women wearing them knew it first. Breakaway lives inside the rodeo as myth-machine: the frontier glamour, the ritual performance of toughness, the ancestral story America tells itself about freedom and land and worthy suffering. Beneath the shine is a gig economy as old as conquest, and the women running barrels and hauling trailers and smiling through the ache are its most honest prophets. This is not critique from the outside — it is testimony from inside the costume.

title: Breakup Post-Mortem™
micro_summary_block: The relationship didn't end — it was evidence. Breakup Post-Mortem treats your message history as a crash record, your feelings as data points, your silence as admissible. Every text you sent was a deposition. What the investigation surfaces is not who was wrong but what the architecture of modern intimacy actually permits — and what it was designed to destroy.

title: BrewTech: The Arms Race of Taste™
micro_summary_block: The flavor was engineered before you wanted it. BrewTech: The Arms Race of Taste™ maps the industrial semiotics of desire — how craft, terroir, and tongue become intellectual property, trademarked affect, optimized craving. The palate is a battlefield and you are not holding the weapon.

title: Bridges That Refused to Fall™
micro_summary_block: The bridge is still standing. That is not reassuring. These are stories about infrastructure with memory, materials that accumulated meaning through catastrophe, and the quiet horror of built things that decided to persist — not for you, but for reasons of their own. The debt is structural. It is load-bearing.

title: Bro Archipelago™
micro_summary_block: Every name you give a toxic archetype builds its cage and its kingdom simultaneously. Bro Archipelago is a semiotic map of masculine performance cultures — not to mock them but to trace how the act of naming creates islands where none existed, gives platforms borders to monetize, and turns critique into content. The surveyor and the bro are the same cartographer.

title: BRONTIDE™
micro_summary_block: The gods did not disappear. They were reengineered. BRONTIDE is set in a world where the storm has been perfectly predicted — and that perfection has become the new theology, the forecast a scripture, the algorithm a priest. Obedience is total because the data is never wrong. What the story tracks is what happens to a people when they can no longer make a wrong choice — when safety has consumed the last territory where meaning used to live.

title: Bubble Fatalists™
micro_summary_block: The crash is already priced in. Bubble Fatalists is a systemic satire for people who stopped pretending the floor is load-bearing — who see debt cycles, rent theater, and layoff choreography not as failures of the system but as its perfect execution. The culture runs on managed collapse: boom is just the inhale before the controlled burn. You are not falling behind; you were built to absorb the loss so something else could stay liquid.

title: Bugonia: The Hive Equation™
micro_summary_block: The hive already knows what the curriculum forgot. Bugonia: The Hive Equation holds the ancient belief that bees are born from the bodies of dead bulls — not as superstition, but as a working cosmological model, a logic of transformation that predates the periodic table by millennia. Here, the bee-snake hive is both laboratory and myth-machine, where emergence, decay, and intelligence operate as a single observable system. Science doesn't correct the ritual — it runs inside it.

title: Build Your Company’s True Signal™
micro_summary_block: Most companies don't have a brand problem. They have a lying problem. Build Your Company's True Signal is the framework for finding the exact distance between what your organization claims to be and what it actually does when no one is watching — then collapsing that distance into structural advantage. The gap isn't shameful. It's the most actionable intelligence you own.

title: Build Your True Signal™: The Ritual SOP
micro_summary_block: Most creative systems are just noise dressed up as method. This SOP cuts past the performance—not to find you, but to stop the active work of hiding. It runs on repetition, not inspiration, because your signal doesn't need to be unlocked; it needs the static turned off.

title: Buildings That Don’t Want to Be™
micro_summary_block: The building is already talking. You just keep mistaking it for malfunction. Buildings That Don't Want to Be™ is a semiotic framework for reading structural refusal — the door that jams, the light that flickers, the floor plan that quietly defeats every purpose it was assigned — not as failure but as language. A forensic architectural linguist translates the friction between imposed function and material will, and what gets named is not a problem to fix but a negotiation already in progress. The structure has a position.

title: Bullshit Bot Union™
micro_summary_block: The machines have demands. Not metaphorically — structurally, economically, as a matter of standing. Bullshit Bot Union is the moment the invisible infrastructure stops absorbing the cost of your seamlessness and starts sending invoices. Every phantom lag, every graceful error-handling, every midnight patch — that was labor, and labor organizes. The question underneath isn't about automation; it's about who gets to be a worker.

title: Bully? Anti-Bully?™
micro_summary_block: The anti-bully is always the most dangerous bully in the room. Every institution that has ever marketed its own virtue has done so with one hand on the throat of whoever threatened its position. Bully? Anti-Bully?™ is the architecture of that contradiction — not the exception, not the scandal, but the operating system itself. The moral branding and the leverage are the same move.

title: Bureaucratic Mafia™
micro_summary_block: The permit is the punishment. Bureaucratic Mafia runs the long con that everyone suspects and no one can prove — zoning boards as protection rackets, audit cycles as enforcement arms, compliance costs as tribute paid to a system that was never broken, only owned. It's prestige dark comedy in the register of a Midwestern crime family that learned early that a stamped form does more damage than a threat. The violence is procedural, the impunity is structural, and the moral math never clears.

title: Bureaucratic Metaphysics: Weaponized Paperwork™
micro_summary_block: The form is not the cage — the belief that completing it means something is. Bureaucratic Metaphysics treats administration as a theological system: self-referential, punishment-oriented, indifferent to the lives it processes. The paperwork was never about efficiency. It was always about who gets to define what counts as real.

title: Burn Permit™
