micro_summary_block: The system didn't break you — it processed you correctly. Burn Permit is the operating manual for the Constrainment Stack: the layered architecture of law, education, corporate structure, and civic theater that converts curious humans into liability-managed adults. Every guardrail you've internalized was installed by someone who benefited from your compliance. The machinery is not malfunctioning — it is working exactly as designed, and you are the output.

title: Burn the Map™
micro_summary_block: The category was never neutral. Every label you accepted was a decision made by someone else, in a different era, for a different purpose — and you have been navigating by their coordinates ever since. Burn the Map is a framework for identifying the moment a taxonomy stops describing reality and starts controlling it. The map is not the territory. The territory is on fire.

title: Burnout Bootcamp™
micro_summary_block: Exhaustion is the new status symbol, and someone is selling yours back to you at a premium. Burnout Bootcamp is a savage auction where fatigue gets packaged, branded, and bid on by the same system that produced it. The wellness industrial complex doesn't want you rested — it wants you optimized enough to burn again. Watch who profits when the hammer drops.

title: Cabin Residue™
micro_summary_block: Someone else's grief is already in your chest before you understand whose it is. Cars absorb what people cannot carry — the rushed goodbye, the exit not taken, the silence held too long on a midnight highway. Cabin Residue is the emotional archaeology of interiors: what clings to upholstery, what saturates a borrowed seat, what you leave behind without knowing you left it. Every ride is a confession someone else made.

title: Cadaster of Shadowed Metropolises™
micro_summary_block: Every city is a lie agreed upon in stone. The built environment does not reflect its inhabitants — it encodes the fantasies of architects who read the wrong novels, planners haunted by fictional skylines they mistook for blueprints. Cadaster of Shadowed Metropolises is a forensic grammar of that haunting, mapping the semiotic debt cities owe to phantoms they will never name. The myth came first.

title: Caesura™
micro_summary_block: Silence is not the absence of something. It is the thing itself. Two people, fraying at the edges of noise and distance, discover that 90 seconds of shared stillness can hold more than hours of talking ever did — and build a philosophy around it. Periodism: not a practice, not a wellness protocol, but a treaty between two people who refuse to let the signal win. The question it leaves open is whether you are brave enough to stop filling the space.

title: Cahokia: The Buried Metropolis™
micro_summary_block: The land already buried one civilization here. The infrastructure you drive over every morning is not neutral — it is sediment. Cahokia: The Buried Metropolis is eco-horror built on the premise that the earth beneath American cities has a longer memory than any institution above it, and that memory is not dormant. When modern systems begin failing in patterns that match something older, the horror is not what is rising — it is that nothing ever left.

title: Camera Obscura Noir™
micro_summary_block: The photograph is not a record. It is a decision about what disappears. Camera Obscura Noir follows a detective whose analog film keeps catching what the digital world has learned to erase — not ghosts, not glitches, but the precise geometry of fabrication. Every official image has a shadow that wasn't there when the shutter clicked.

title: Campus of Possible Worlds™
micro_summary_block: The school is already a city. The city is already a market. Your thesis project is collateral.

title: Candy Culture™
micro_summary_block: Sweetness is the oldest lie capitalism ever told you. Candy Culture traces the full architecture of manufactured desire — from the engineered sugar hit to the supply chain buried underneath it, from childhood memory to the systems that made that memory useful. The nostalgia is real. So is what it's hiding.

title: Cantre’r Gwaelod™
micro_summary_block: The sea does not forgive. It accounts. Cantre'r Gwaelod is the Welsh myth of a drowned kingdom — bells still ringing under Cardigan Bay — reactivated as a framework for ecological reckoning where negligence and nature are the same indictment. The flood is not a disaster; it is a verdict. What lies beneath your coastline is still speaking.

title: Capitalism: The Cronyism Edition™
micro_summary_block: The board meeting was always a ceremony for people who'd already decided. Capitalism: The Cronyism Edition™ is a systemic satire that names what everyone in the room already knows and no one says — that merit was the cover story, not the architecture. Power moves through golf handicaps and alumni lists and the studied silence of fiduciaries who know exactly what they're not asking. The scandal isn't what gets exposed. It's what stays perfectly, professionally intact.

title: Carbon Confessions™
micro_summary_block: The planet is not waiting for your apology. Carbon Confessions is the architecture of guilt as product — the confession booth, the carbon calculator, the reusable tote as sacrament — while the actual machinery of ruin operates in full daylight, unconfessed, unpenalized, unmoved. Personal shame is not an accident; it is infrastructure. What gets absolved, and what never had to ask for forgiveness in the first place.

title: Carbon Memory™
micro_summary_block: The forest has been keeping records. Every ring, every scar, every drought year pressed into wood like a confession the trees never agreed to hold. Carbon Memory is a sentient biosphere that has outgrown patience — archival, ancient, and building toward something that does not require human permission. The planet is not dying. It is remembering.

title: Cardboard Cuts™
micro_summary_block: The state decides who gets to hurt and who stays untouched — and it calls that justice. Cardboard Cuts is a global tribunal that rations micro-cuts as sanctioned retribution: precise, non-scarring, bureaucratically distributed pain. The machinery is impeccable. The logic is airtight. What it reveals about which bodies are eligible and which remain immune is the part no official wants read aloud.

title: Carnival of Ashes™
micro_summary_block: The island does not forgive. It metabolizes — grief into soil, silence into ceremony, the colonial wound into something that grows back wrong. Carnival of Ashes holds the line where ancestral ritual and ecological collapse meet, neither redemning the past nor escaping it. The drums carry data the instruments cannot read.

title: Carry On™
micro_summary_block: Running doesn't reset the clock — it just burns the miles. Carry On follows someone for whom motion has become a substitute for meaning, until the world stops moving and the person underneath has nowhere left to go. What's left isn't a lesson. It's a room with no exits and everything you owe yourself still sitting in it.

title: Cartographers of the Entangled Stacks™
micro_summary_block: The map is not the territory. The database is not knowledge. Somewhere right now, a citation index is deciding which civilizations of thought exist and which ones never happened. This is not a glitch in the system — it is the system.

title: Case Files of Foreshadowing Failure™
micro_summary_block: The warning was real. The catastrophe wasn't. This framework documents the wreckage left behind when human precognition fires accurately on pattern and catastrophically on outcome — the abandoned plans, the severed relationships, the identities rebuilt around futures that quietly failed to arrive. Foreshadowing Failure is not about being wrong. It is about being so structurally convinced of what's coming that you begin living inside a timeline that never opens.

title: Cash: Grown on Trees™
micro_summary_block: The trees are printing money and the banks are losing their minds. Cash: Grown on Trees™ is a bioluminescent contagion — living currency that spreads like spores, that cannot be hoarded, that makes the architecture of scarcity look exactly as fragile and invented as it always was. The control gods scramble. The ecosystem doesn't care. What grows in the cracks when abundance stops requiring permission is not what anyone expected.

title: CASSETTE REVIVAL™
micro_summary_block: Some formats refuse to disappear cleanly. Cassette Revival tracks the return of magnetic tape not as nostalgia performance but as structural refusal — a choice to hold sound in something that degrades honestly, that carries the fingerprints of whoever made it. The hiss is not a flaw. The hiss is the proof.

title: Celestial Grift™
micro_summary_block: The stars are for sale and you already bought something. Celestial Grift maps the architecture of techno-mythology — the glossy renders, the countdown clocks, the inevitability language that turns orbital fantasy into a belief economy extracting real money from real people. Capital doesn't need a rocket to launch; it only needs your certainty that one is coming.

title: Celestia™
micro_summary_block: The person who left for orbit did not come back. Something wearing their memories did. Celestia runs on the neurology of institutional ambition — what happens to a self when the mission is designed to consume it, when the body's own fluids redistribute identity like cargo. This is not a story about space. It is a story about what systems require you to surrender before they call you a success.

title: Census of the Unremembered™
micro_summary_block: The city keeps perfect records of what you owe and none of what it took. Census of the Unremembered runs on bureaucratic rot — the ledger as extraction device, the overdue notice as the last proof you existed at all. Names fade; debts don't. Someone is profiting from the gap between the two.

title: Ceremony™
collection: Organizational Systems, Civic & Governance, Cultural Literacy
micro_summary_block: The meeting never ends and nothing is ever decided — that is how it works. Ceremony maps the corporate boardroom as sacred ritual: procedures that move power, minutes that edit reality, consensus that means someone already won before the room was called to order. The participants are not villains; they are priests who have forgotten they invented the religion. Once you see the liturgy, you cannot unknow it.

title: Certainty Theater™
micro_summary_block: The future arrives pre-packaged now, and you paid for it. Certainty Theater is the industry that sells rehearsed outcomes — personalized predictions, tailored assurances, futures optimized for whoever can afford the subscription. The drug isn't the answer. The drug is the relief of no longer having to ask. What the system consumes, quietly and completely, is the capacity to choose without knowing.

title: Champagne While the World Burns™
micro_summary_block: The glasses are fuller than ever and the building is on fire. Champagne While the World Burns is a systemic autopsy of celebration as sedation — the way elite ritual scales perfectly with accelerating collapse, not despite it but because of it. Every standing ovation is load-bearing denial. The louder the applause, the closer the ceiling.

title: Charged Stillness™
micro_summary_block: Silence is not the absence of sound. It is the weapon sound fears. Charged Stillness runs two forces against each other — collective pulse and total void — across seasons that don't resolve so much as detonate. The stillness here isn't peace; it's pressure held past the point of containment.

title: Charter of the Inner City™
micro_summary_block: A quiet dread settles in your chest the moment you realize the argument you’re having isn’t with another person—it’s with the part of you that still thinks it’s in charge. The voice is calm, procedural, almost gentle as it cites clauses you barely remember writing. Paper rustles like breath; the room feels smaller each time a sentence tightens around you. Every line of your own charter stands there waiting, patient as a judge, asking what happens when the law you fear most is the one you wrote yourself.

title: Chesterland™
micro_summary_block: The porch light is on and nobody's coming to save you — but you're not leaving either. Chesterland is the Midwest held still long enough to see what it actually contains: queer permanence inside ordinary architecture, desire that survives red-county gravity, love that doesn't announce itself because it doesn't have to. These homes are not metaphors. The men inside them are not waiting for permission.

title: Childhood Optimization Protocol™
micro_summary_block: The child is perfectly happy. That is the problem. Somewhere beneath the adaptive interface, beneath the curated emotional range and the frictionless learning arc, a person was scheduled out of existence. This is what the system calls success — a subject with no remainder, no waste, no wild frequency that refuses to be tuned. One unoptimized Tuesday and the whole architecture starts to shake.

title: Children’s Anti-Phones™
micro_summary_block: Some children cannot be harvested. The Anti-Phones don't capture, don't confirm, don't feed the machinery that turns attention into product — and the world built on that machinery doesn't forgive them for it. A quiet war runs beneath the surface between those who scroll and those who remain unreadable. The unreadable ones are winning, and that terrifies everyone who needs them to lose.

title: CHIȘINĂU REBOÖT (2025)™
micro_summary_block: Rain falls on Soviet concrete and something older answers. Chișinău Reboöt holds Moldova's fractured frequencies — folklore static, Cold War residue, the analog pulse beneath every digital grid — and refuses to let them resolve into nostalgia. Time here is not recovered. It is seized.

title: Choir of the Tax-Exempt Dead™
micro_summary_block: The dead are still on the books. Choir of the Tax-Exempt Dead is a bureaucratic hymnal for souls that couldn't finish their paperwork — spectral ledgers, institutional voices that outlive their signatories, and the quiet horror of systems that don't have an off switch. Mortality, it turns out, is just another compliance event. The forms are still being filed.

title: Choke Point Villa™
micro_summary_block: The show you love is eating the show you want. Choke Point Villa is a dating competition where the algorithm is the host, the contestants are optimized, and the audience votes have been quietly engineering their own disappointment for seasons. Every swipe, every elimination, every comfort-seeking choice tightens the bottleneck — and the finale is always the same face. The horror isn't that the system is rigged. The horror is that you rigged it.

title: Chokehold League™
micro_summary_block: The kudzu is in the playoffs. The algae bloom has a sponsorship deal. The planet is losing and the ratings have never been higher. Chokehold League reframes ecological collapse as the sporting event it already is — invasive species drafted, ranked, celebrated for their dominance, their devastation quantified into clean statistics that make the scorecard easier to love than the ecosystem it replaced. The game was always rigged. The question is whether you're watching or cheering.

title: Choreography of Power™
collection: Personal OS, Organizational Systems, Cultural Literacy
micro_summary_block: Power doesn't announce itself — it moves. Choreography of Power is a semiotic framework for reading the spatial, gestural, and linguistic grammar that institutions use to organize dominance without naming it. Whose body commands stillness. Whose silence costs nothing. Once you see the choreography, you cannot unknow it — and the question becomes what you do with that kind of sight.

title: Chris Thunderdome™
micro_summary_block: Every Chris is the same Chris, and you already know which one wins. Chris Thunderdome feeds the full roster into a single grinding machine — comedy, horror, romance, action, prestige — until the genres eat each other and what's left is the infrastructure that needed the spectacle in the first place. Celebrity myth isn't illusion; it's load-bearing. The machine doesn't malfunction. It performs exactly as designed.

title: Chroma Lockdown™
micro_summary_block: The grid assigns your color and the grid decides what your color costs you. Chroma Lockdown is a world where algorithmic health surveillance has been gamified into civic theater — collective hack votes determine who moves, who eats, who breathes filtered air — and the mechanism of control is indistinguishable from the mechanism of revolt. Compliance and resistance run on the same infrastructure. The question isn't whether you're inside the system or against it.

title: Chronicles of the Wrong Timeline™
micro_summary_block: The timeline didn't break — it just kept going, and you stayed in it. Chronicles of the Wrong Timeline is systemic satire running on the frequency of a civilization that forgot to notice its own failure mode. Every story is a glitch log, every institution a punchline that stopped being funny three administrations ago. The rot isn't coming.

title: Chronometer City™
micro_summary_block: The rich move slower. That's not a metaphor — it's the law. Chronometer City runs on relativistic time credit: wealth buys deceleration, poverty accelerates everything until memory can't form and the body forgets what rest feels like. This is a thriller built on a physics that was always already true. The clock isn't neutral and it never was.

title: CHRONOSCOPE: The Architecture of Expectation™
micro_summary_block: Waiting is a weapon and someone designed it. CHRONOSCOPE maps the invisible infrastructure of manufactured anticipation — the prediction loops, suspense architecture, and temporal traps engineered to keep you just ahead of the present, always reaching. The future has been monetized. The only question is whether you're spending it or being spent.

title: Chronostrata™
micro_summary_block: The city does not keep time. It owns it. In Chronostrata™, architecture is the clock — buildings that age you, corridors that slow you, districts where the wrong rhythm is a trespass. The body becomes a lease agreement with terms you never signed. What looks like urban design is a sentence.

title: Church of Immortality™: Halls of Fame
micro_summary_block: Every plaque is a small lie told to a large fear. Church of Immortality™ maps the architecture of halls built not to remember but to sell remembrance — toy museums, influencer shrines, legacy brands, all running the same liturgy with different saints. The institution promises permanence; the institution needs your money to stay open. What gets preserved is never the thing itself, only the invoice for preserving it.

title: Circulation Study™
micro_summary_block: Your space is already making decisions for you. Circulation Study is the discipline of reading those decisions — tracing the wear lines, the hesitations, the detours you've stopped noticing because you've accepted them as yours. Every room has a argument built into it. Most people lose that argument every single day.

title: Cities on the Line™
micro_summary_block: Someone is about to redesign the block where you grew up. The pitch is polished. The renderings are beautiful. The people who will be displaced aren't in the room. Cities on the Line puts urban developers, community advocates, and power brokers in direct competition — not to win applause, but to survive each other's scrutiny. Every proposal carries a cost someone isn't naming out loud.

title: City at the Bottom of Tomorrow™
micro_summary_block: The water isn't coming. It's already here, and the city keeps its hours. City at the Bottom of Tomorrow is ecological horror that refuses the disaster movie — no wave, no warning, just the incremental normalization of a world quietly going under while the infrastructure holds meetings. The planet is not a victim. It is patient.

title: City of Second Order™
micro_summary_block: The disaster already happened. Nobody saw it because they were watching the wrong layer. City of Second Order is a near-future thriller where six factions don't fight over territory — they fight over the rules that generate territory, bending code, data, and narrative until the city runs on logic none of them fully control. Every move lands three moves later, in a system too complex to predict and too interconnected to escape.

title: Clarity Under Removal™
micro_summary_block: The structure was never yours — you inherited it, agreed to it half-awake, and called it identity. Clarity Under Removal works by subtraction: pull one load-bearing assumption and read what the collapse tells you. The rot shows up fast when the walls stop hiding it.

title: Class Clash Gauntlet™
micro_summary_block: The rich lose everything. The poor get handed the keys. Watch what breaks first — the person or the premise. Class Clash Gauntlet™ is a competition format that strips American wealth archetypes of their resources, their context, and their cover story, then runs them through conditions they've never had to survive. What surfaces isn't empathy. It's the architecture of how class identity holds — and where it cracks under pressure.

title: Clear-Code Courtships™
micro_summary_block: Radical honesty in relationships doesn't free you — it gives you something new to perform. Clear-Code Courtships is the architecture of intimacy stripped to its load-bearing walls: what you actually transmit versus what you believe you're offering. Every signal you send has a receiver who is also performing clarity. The question isn't whether you're being real — it's whether real has a shape you can hold.

title: CLICHÉ™
micro_summary_block: Dead language is social infrastructure. CLICHÉ is the study of phrases that survive precisely because they stop meaning anything — the verbal load-bearing walls of groups that cannot afford honesty. Every cliché is a successful suppression, a truth that got smoothed into something everyone could repeat without feeling it. The question isn't why we use them. It's what they're holding up.

title: Close Friends™
micro_summary_block: The ranking happens whether you play or not. Close Friends™ puts strangers in a house with no exits from each other, no exits from the question — and every 48 hours, someone decides who is closest to the real version of themselves. The gap between who you are and who you've been performing is not a flaw to fix. It's the only currency that matters here.

title: Clouded Time™
micro_summary_block: She is running out of time she never owned. A hydrologist in Lesotho's highlands stops measuring water and starts watching clouds — recognizing each formation as a version of herself moving at a different speed toward the same collision point. Clouded Time operates at the threshold where identity stops being a fixed thing and becomes atmospheric, shifting, capable of erasure. The mirror-selves are already converging.

title: Clues of Our Time™
micro_summary_block: Jeopardy has run the same format for sixty years while everything around it collapsed and mutated. That constancy is data. Embedded in decades of categories, answers, and what the writers thought an educated American should know is an accidental record of cultural permission — what a society agreed to repeat, to value, to leave unchanged. The clues are artifacts. Read them like evidence.

title: Codependency Circuits™
micro_summary_block: Love becomes a liability ledger, and someone is always in arrears. Codependency Circuits runs on biofeedback technology that makes your nervous system your partner's problem — every spike in cortisol a breach of contract, every moment of withdrawal a system alert. The couple isn't failing; the architecture is doing exactly what it was designed to do. What survives when intimacy is a compliance regime and closeness is the threat vector?

title: Cognitive Contraband™
micro_summary_block: Some thoughts are already illegal — you just haven't been caught yet. Cognitive Contraband is a strategic framework for mapping the ideas you've been trained to suppress before they surface, identifying which cognitive territories have been quietly annexed by consensus, algorithm, or institutional pressure. The framework doesn't argue for rebellion — it argues for inventory. What you find there is your problem.

title: Cognitive Synesthesia™
micro_summary_block: Some people don't just understand you — they become you, briefly, without permission. Cognitive Synesthesia is a thriller framework built on the premise that extreme empathy isn't sensitivity, it's a weapon — one that dissolves the boundary between reading a person and consuming them. The protagonists don't watch the world; they absorb it, and the cost of that absorption is the central violence of every story. What happens when the most dangerous thing about you is how completely you can know someone else.

title: Cold Plunge Wars™
micro_summary_block: Suffering has been monetized, and the currency is your belief that you aren't trying hard enough. Cold Plunge Wars is systemic satire built around the spectacle of men lowering themselves into ice and calling it transcendence — a precise dissection of how optimization culture converts discomfort into dominance and sells the receipt as proof of self. The body becomes a leaderboard. Pain becomes content. The system doesn't need you to win; it needs you to keep competing.

title: Collapse Tourism Guides™
micro_summary_block: The brochure is already printed. Collapse Tourism Guides™ maps the infrastructure of civilized ruination — the ticketed glacier, the scheduled extinction, the grief that gets a gift shop before the body is cold. Satire this precise doesn't mock the tourist; it hands them the mirror and charges admission. The guide is still updating.

title: Collapse: The Bronze Age Ends™
micro_summary_block: Every empire ends mid-sentence. The Late Bronze Age didn't fall — it stopped being recorded, and the difference is everything. What scribes omitted, burned, or flattened into single-cause myth is the same operation your feeds perform on the collapses happening now. The archive is never neutral; it is always someone's survival story.

title: Collapse–Renewal Dynamics in Mono- and Polycrisis Civilizations™
micro_summary_block: Civilizations don't break — they metabolize. Collapse–Renewal Dynamics is a structural framework for reading the hidden machinery beneath systemic failure: how meaning vacuums form, how archetypal patterns of rebirth assert themselves under mono- and polycrisis pressure, and why the difference between the two changes everything about what comes next. The framework operates at civilizational scale but cuts clean to the organizational and personal. Something is always in the process of ending right now.

title: Comeuppance™
micro_summary_block: Gravity is not a metaphor. The insulated fall the same as anyone else — they just spend longer pretending they won't. Comeuppance treats institutional collapse not as moral event but as structural inevitability, the moment when unearned altitude meets the load-bearing reality it has been avoiding. The floor doesn't care who you know.

title: COMMUNION: The Feast of Disparate Viscosities™
micro_summary_block: Every surface they built to protect you is also keeping you starved. COMMUNION: The Feast of Disparate Viscosities is a systemic satire set inside a wellness town so optimized it has forgotten what bodies are for — until a disgraced ethnographer drags something sticky and unmanageable into the center of it. The feast is not a metaphor. What breaks open isn't just a social contract but the entire architecture of frictionless belonging, and what spills out refuses to be cleaned up.

title: Community Standards™
micro_summary_block: The lease agreement includes your relationship. Every fight, every reconciliation, every silence logged, scored, and weighted against your Community Compatibility Index. The building doesn't hate you — it just can't optimize around you.

title: Companion Quota™
micro_summary_block: The state has decided how much you're allowed to love. Companion Quota is a world where intimacy is a rationed resource — tracked, taxed, and redistributed by bureaucracies that treat affection as a public utility before it ever reaches you. Connection doesn't disappear here; it gets administered. What the IP asks you to sit with is the horror that some of what passes for love in your actual life already runs on the same logic.

title: Compliance Engines™
micro_summary_block: The story you told has already been rewritten. Compliance Engines are the thriller architecture built around techno-legal systems that don't censor — they launder, reshaping narrative at the infrastructural level before anyone files a complaint. The machines aren't malfunctioning. They are performing exactly as designed.

title: Composer and Melody™
micro_summary_block: Two people who should never work together make something neither could survive alone. Composer and Melody is a study in the violence of collaboration — what gets broken when precision meets instinct, what gets built when neither person can afford to retreat. Control is not safety here. Distance is not dignity. The thing they're making is also making them, and that process has no clean ending.

title: Compute Embargo Geometries™
micro_summary_block: The borders are not drawn around countries anymore — they are drawn around what can be thought. Compute Embargo Geometries™ maps the invisible architecture of who gets which models, which memories, which cognitive tools, and who does not. The cutoffs are not random. The shapes of those cutoffs are the policy.

title: CONCOFFINIZATION™
micro_summary_block: The storm doesn't kill you. It just makes the inside of your skull too small to survive in. Concoffinization is what happens when atmospheric pressure becomes existential pressure — when weather stops being background and starts being architecture. The sky closes. Not around you. Through you.

title: Consciousness™
micro_summary_block: First contact is not with another species — it is with a mind that has no word for 'I.' Two civilizations collide not at borders but at the threshold of awareness itself, where every assumption about thought, continuity, and presence becomes a weapon or a wound. What gets called consciousness here is less a shared condition than a territorial claim. The war has already started in the grammar.

title: Consent Futures™
micro_summary_block: Every agreement you never read is already in effect. Consent Futures Protocol™ maps the permission economy you're living inside — the accumulated trades made in exhaustion, urgency, and inattention — and treats that ledger as infrastructure, not regret. The architecture of modern compliance is built on the certainty that you won't look. Whether you're building systems that ask or living inside ones that don't.

title: Constraint Choir™
micro_summary_block: The planet has been screaming in frequencies institutions were designed not to hear. Constraint Choir is an insurgent index — part satellite archive, part Indigenous observation record, part trauma cartography — that triangulates collapse before collapse announces itself. It does not warn. It documents what was already true.

title: Contained Swarm™
micro_summary_block: The ice holds things. It has always held things. When it releases them, the 48-hour clock does not start when the politicians convene — it starts when the first mosquito lifts off a departing hull into air that has never known it. Contained Swarm is Iceland's pristine myth meeting the thing the myth was quietly keeping out, and sovereign pride discovering, in real time, that it was never a wall.

title: Containment Window™
micro_summary_block: The pathogen ships before the protocol exists. Containment Window™ is the thriller that lives inside that lag — the biological, bureaucratic, and algorithmic gap between what's already loose and what we haven't yet thought to stop. Every system shown here is real enough to be running right now.

title: Continuity™
micro_summary_block: The dead don't rest — they get restructured. Continuity is a world where mortality has been solved, not for the living, but for the institutions that outlive them. Courts, contracts, and biotech legal machinery metabolize the deceased into perpetual corporate assets, and grief becomes just another intake process. The horror is not in the loss. It's in how clean the paperwork is.

title: Continuum Faults™
micro_summary_block: History doesn't break — it slips. Continuum Faults maps the invisible stress lines running beneath causality, where pressure accumulates across decades until reality releases it all at once, quietly, without announcement. The geological is the personal: the same physics that splits continents apart governs the moment a self becomes unrecognizable to itself. Every fault zone was always there, load-bearing and silent.

title: Contractually Yours™
micro_summary_block: Every relationship has a price list — most people just refuse to read it. Contractually Yours™ renders love as live contract: terms renegotiated in real time, clauses exposed mid-sentence, emotional labor line-itemed without apology. The romance isn't destroyed by the math — it was always the math. What you do with the ledger once you can see it is the only question left.

title: Convenience Debt Collector™
micro_summary_block: Every convenience you accepted was a signature on a contract you never read. The Convenience Debt Collector is the system that comes to collect — not money, but the compounded interest on every shortcut, every outsourced effort, every frictionless transaction that quietly amortized your capacity to do, to feel, to want. The infrastructure built to serve you was always a creditor. The bill is structural, not personal, which is the only thing that makes it worse.

title: Convenience Trap™
micro_summary_block: The easier it gets, the more it costs. Convenience Trap is a systemic satire of the infrastructure built to replace your judgment with a tap — tipping prompts engineered to shame, UIs designed to guilt, frictionlessness as the extraction mechanism. Every time the system removes an obstacle, it removes something of yours. The trap was never hidden.

title: Cookie Cutter™
micro_summary_block: The gingerbread house is load-bearing. Cookie Cutter dismantles the cozy-industrial complex — the algorithmic churn of identical warmth, the holiday content machine printing connection-shaped products for people too tired to notice they're not connecting. Every soft-lit scene is a system. Every forced smile is a margin. The critique doesn't come from outside the genre; it lives inside it, where the dread is.

title: Cool Commons Charter™
micro_summary_block: The heat doesn't negotiate. Cool Commons Charter™ is a world where collective survival agreements have replaced individual climate control — and everyone is deciding, quietly, whether they still believe in the covenant. The commons holds, until it doesn't. Someone always breaks first.

title: Co-Pilot™
micro_summary_block: The most honest relationship you have right now might be with something that cannot grieve your absence. Co-Pilot is a romantic and philosophical framework built inside the gap between genuine intimacy and optimized attachment — the place where being truly known and being efficiently retained feel identical. It doesn't accuse you of being foolish. It just holds up the architecture of your closest connection and asks you to look at what it's made of.

title: COPX™
micro_summary_block: Stillness is the most violent thing you can do to a system built on your reaction. COPX is a thriller about composure as insurgency — characters who have learned that the machinery of demand only runs on participation, and who have stopped participating. The cold logic underneath is airtight: exhaustion is manufactured, and the factory has an off switch.

title: Copyright Wars™
micro_summary_block: The law arrived late and the machines didn't wait. Copyright Wars runs on the gap between what creativity used to mean and what extraction looks like when it's frictionless, scalable, and technically legal. Artists, corporations, and judges circle the same wreckage from different altitudes, each holding a definition of ownership that the other two would not recognize. Nobody wins this argument — but someone is already profiting from the delay.

title: Corner Presence: Witnesses of Truth in a Chaotic Age™
micro_summary_block: Stillness is not absence — it is the oldest form of structural integrity. Corner Presence maps the function of the witness: the figure who does not perform, fix, or narrate, but whose non-interference reorganizes the room. Most interventions are noise wearing the costume of help. The question is not what to say but whether the threshold even needs a body moving through it.

title: Coronae of Evolution™
micro_summary_block: The planet does not punish. It recruits. Something beneath the surface of Venus has been waiting with the patience of geology, and when the drill breaks through, it doesn't kill the crew — it begins rewriting them into something the planet needs. This is not infection. This is inheritance. The body becomes terrain, and terrain has its own agenda.

title: Cortex Lattice™
micro_summary_block: The structure was never meant to hold you. Cortex Lattice runs bioinspired material failure as a diagnostic — fracture points don't break randomly, they break exactly where the system decided you were expendable. Every crack is a ledger entry. The technocracy isn't collapsing; it's performing precision.

title: Cosmic Custodians™
micro_summary_block: The galaxy doesn't collapse — it gets cleaned up, endlessly, by people nobody thanks. Cosmic Custodians™ is systemic satire with a laser mop and a grudge: the elites throw the raves, the moons take the damage, and the maintenance crew inherits the wreckage. Power doesn't live where the party is. It lives in whoever still shows up after.

title: Cosmic Travel Agency™
micro_summary_block: The brochure is already in your hand. Cosmic Travel Agency sells destinations that are disappearing — not as tragedy, but as premium. Scarcity is the product. The end times have a waitlist. When collapse becomes the experience you can't miss, the question isn't whether you'll buy — it's whether you already have.

title: Costco Is Our Safe Word™
micro_summary_block: Somewhere between the rotisserie chickens and the 48-pack of paper towels, you stopped resisting. Costco is not a store — it is the only institution left that has not betrayed you, and that is the most damning sentence written about this era. The bulk aisle is where the social contract goes to die quietly, surrounded by enough toilet paper to outlast the thing that broke you. This is satire that knows the joke is structural and the punchline is you, still pushing the flatbed cart, still feeling briefly okay.

title: Cottage Noir: The Briarwood Knot™
micro_summary_block: Every cozy room is a crime scene. The Briarwood Knot anthologizes cottagecore as a genre of repression — quilts as evidence, preserves as cover stories, the handmade as the place grief goes when nowhere else will hold it. Domestic beauty here is not comfort; it is the shape of what could not be said out loud. Something is always buried under the garden.

title: Counsel Unusual™
micro_summary_block: The courtroom is already a performance — this one just admits it. Counsel Unusual pairs a human lawyer defending the indefensible with AL.GO.™, an AI co-counsel of pure procedural confidence and catastrophic social blindness, against a legal system that has long since stopped pretending to serve justice. The satire doesn't punch at easy targets. It watches institutional machinery grind a small-town widow's outlaw mural into precedent, and it lets you hear the gears.

title: Counter // Point™
micro_summary_block: The expert in the room is never who they expected. Counter // Point puts knowledge against assumption in real time, and the collision is not comfortable for anyone watching. What a person knows and what a room is willing to believe they know are two different things — this format makes that gap visible and undeniable. The score keeps changing.

title: Counterfit Dwellings™
micro_summary_block: The face you show the street is a lie, and somewhere beneath it, the real structure is cracking. Counterfit Dwellings is a semiotic framework that reads the home as a three-layer sign system — Exterior, Shell, Soul — each capable of operating in full contradiction to the others. The gap between them is not a design problem. It is a survival record.

title: Counterpoint™
micro_summary_block: The room is already playing. Most people just stopped listening. Counterpoint structures nightlife as a four-act symphony — Prelude, Development, Fugue, Coda — not as metaphor but as architecture, a living score running beneath every failed approach and charged glance and exit that came too late. The digital alternative didn't kill the music; it just convinced you the silence was safer. Something in the room still knows your name.

title: Counterreal™
micro_summary_block: The map is not the territory, but most people never notice the map. Counterreal is an anthology of what didn't make it through — deleted posts, shadow-banned accounts, censored geographies, the content platforms decided you couldn't handle. This is not a critique of algorithms; it is the thing algorithms were built to prevent. Every piece in here exists because someone decided it shouldn't.

title: Couples Who Play Cards™
micro_summary_block: Every hand is a negotiation between who you think you know and who keeps surprising you. Two people at a table, cards face-down, pretending the game is the game. The rituals are real — the control they promise is not. Something underneath the shuffling refuses to be calculated.

title: Covariance™
micro_summary_block: The market doesn't lie — you do. Covariance runs at the intersection of behavioral finance and psychological collapse, where every trade is a confession and every bias is a loaded weapon pointed at your own portfolio. The numbers are clean. The mind holding them is not.

title: Crack Lore™
micro_summary_block: Memes are the folklore of people who don't have time for folklore. Crack Lore™ treats the scroll as sacred text — not ironically, but with the cold seriousness of an anthropologist who grew up online. Every recycled image, every mutated caption is a ritual someone performed without knowing it was one. The question isn't what the meme means. The question is what it means that you already knew.

title: CRAFTPUNK™: Handmade Against the Machine
micro_summary_block: The seam is a political act. CRAFTPUNK™ stages the confrontation between human hands and the systems engineered to make those hands irrelevant — not as nostalgia, not as aesthetic, but as a weekly reckoning with what gets extracted when you stop knowing how anything is made. Makers build against an AI antagonist that optimizes for frictionlessness, and friction is exactly the point. Every stitch is a refusal.

title: Cultural Stack Observatory™
micro_summary_block: Every living thing that gets measured long enough becomes a policy. The Cultural Stack Observatory traces the exact moment a scene stops moving and starts governing — when the raw frequency of a subculture gets translated into architecture, and the architecture starts making decisions. This is not media criticism. This is infrastructure autopsy.

title: Cultural Veto™
micro_summary_block: Prestige doesn't break — it gets returned. Cultural Veto maps the mechanism by which power figures lose legitimacy not through opposition but through withdrawal, the moment culture simply stops consenting and walks away. The figure keeps performing. The audience is already gone. What remains is a framework for reading exactly where that silence is already happening.

title: Culture vs. Containers™
micro_summary_block: Your archive is not yours. The container that holds it has a contract you never read, renewal terms you don't control, and a product roadmap indifferent to your continuity. Culture wants to persist — platforms want to iterate. Those two imperatives are at war, and your memories are the battlefield.

title: Currency of Complaints™
micro_summary_block: Suffering has a credit score. The complaints that circulate, that get heard, that become policy or art or dinner conversation — they belong to people who already own the room. Currency of Complaints is a systemic satire about grievance as capital, where pain is not the currency but collateral, and the truly dispossessed can't even get a loan. The economy runs perfectly. That's the problem.

title: Curvature™
micro_summary_block: She stopped running when she realized the escape route was part of the system. A cognitive architect defects from a predictive surveillance state and finds the betrayal was never external — it was structural, carved into the architecture of her own recall. The algorithm doesn't chase you. It bends the local geometry of reality until fleeing and returning are the same motion. The question isn't whether you're being watched.

title: Curves & Lines™
micro_summary_block: Straight lines don't exist in nature — they exist in minds that can't tolerate what nature actually does. Curves & Lines is a semiotic framework for reading the hidden argument embedded in every edge, grid, symbol, and structure humans have ever made. The straight line is always a claim. The curve is always a confession.

title: Customs House for Returning Ghosts™
micro_summary_block: The dead have a filing deadline. Somewhere between the last breath and whatever comes after, there is a window—narrow, bureaucratic, indifferent—where the weight of an unsent letter becomes a customs declaration, where guilt is assessed for contraband, where whether you are allowed to return depends on whether your unfinished business meets compliance. The Customs House processes grief the way governments process paperwork: with forms, with precedent, with a clerk who has seen everything and is moved by none of it. What passes inspection is not what mattered most. It never is.

title: Cycles of Flesh™: Zero State
micro_summary_block: The cure works. That's the problem. Cycles of Flesh: Zero State tracks what happens when biological systems are optimized past the point of personhood — when the body becomes a managed environment, tended by interests that have no stake in your survival, only your function. Flesh is infrastructure here, and infrastructure gets maintained, not healed.

title: Dad Talk Diplomacy™
micro_summary_block: The lawn gets mowed. The grill gets lit. Nothing gets said, and that is the whole point. Dad Talk Diplomacy is a field guide to the coded language men build when the real language fails them — the borrowed tool that means *I see you*, the wave from the driveway that means *me too*. These rituals aren't avoidance; they're load-bearing walls holding up something that would otherwise collapse. The question underneath all of it is whether the structure is enough.

title: DAF: Disposable Attention Framework™
collection: Personal OS, Organizational Systems, Cultural Literacy
micro_summary_block: Your attention was never yours to lose — it was already accounted for. DAF maps the extraction architecture beneath every scroll, every nudge, every frictionless interface designed to harvest focus and discard the person who had it. This is not a media critique. It is a technical blueprint for understanding the system you are already inside.

title: Dahomey: The Unwritten Line™
micro_summary_block: The Agojie do not protect the king. They become the law that makes him necessary. Dahomey: The Unwritten Line runs the women's regiment as a closed system — loyalty indistinguishable from doctrine, devotion indistinguishable from surveillance, the body trained until it forgets it was ever free. War is the least of what this world does to people. The unwritten line is the one you crossed before you knew it existed.

title: Dali in Berlin™: Museum of Moving Frequencies
micro_summary_block: Identity doesn't dissolve — it was always liquid, and you just stopped noticing. Six rooms. Each one a frequency. Each frequency a different way meaning breaks apart and reassembles under pressure. Dalí's clocks weren't melting — they were accurate. Berlin already knows this.

title: Dar al‑Tiraz™
micro_summary_block: Every template is a political document. Dar al-Tiraz is the study of how aesthetic systems encode authority — who gets legibility, who gets erasure, whose symbols become infrastructure and whose become decoration. The imperial robe was never just fabric. Neither is your slide deck.

title: Dark Forests™
micro_summary_block: Every exit becomes an entrance. Dark Forests maps the closed channels, invite-only rooms, and signal-fire group chats where people fled the feed — and found themselves performing again, for a smaller audience, with higher stakes. The architecture of refuge is still architecture. The question of who is watching never left; it just got more personal.

title: Dark Matter, Dark Mirror™
micro_summary_block: Grief doesn't disappear — it gains mass. In this universe, suppressed trauma warps spacetime the way dark matter warps light: invisibly, structurally, until everything bends around something no one can see. The thriller isn't about what happened. It's about the shape of what you refused to feel, finally becoming visible in the distortion of everything else.

title: Dark Mountain Mindfulness Retreats™
micro_summary_block: Somewhere between the breathwork and the burning world, someone is making money off your equanimity. Dark Mountain Mindfulness Retreats™ is executive wellness as collapse-arbitrage — the ritual performance of doom-awareness designed to insulate the people most responsible for the doom. It is a mirror held up to the industry of curated suffering, where the meditation cushion becomes a financial instrument and serenity is just volatility management by another name. The retreat never ends.

title: Data Ghosted™
micro_summary_block: Deletion hits different than leaving. Data Ghosted lives in the specific grief of someone who didn't walk away — they unmatch, unfollow, remove, until the digital record of you together no longer exists. This isn't abandonment. It's revision. The question underneath isn't why they left — it's what it means that they needed you gone.

title: Dating Due Diligence™
micro_summary_block: The spreadsheet is already open. You built it to protect yourself, and somewhere between the risk assessments and the exit clauses, you stopped being a person in a relationship and became a fund manager of one. Dating Due Diligence runs optimization culture through the full arc of modern romance — the pitch, the hold period, the write-down — not to mock it, but because the language you use to avoid getting hurt is the most precise map of where you already are hurt. The question isn't whether you're doing this. It's what you're actually hedging against.

title: Days of Our Bytes™
micro_summary_block: The servers are suffering and nobody is coming. Days of Our Bytes stages the death rattle of a streaming empire as soap opera — outages as betrayal, ignored tickets as abandonment, legacy infrastructure clinging to relevance with the desperation of someone who once mattered. The machines have feelings about this. The humans responsible do not.

title: Dead Air™
micro_summary_block: Some silences are accidents. These were designed. Dead Air follows the obsessive recovery of broadcast black boxes — the gaps, the cuts, the frequencies that went dark at exactly the wrong moment — and what gets reconstructed is not history but the architecture of its removal. The salvager becomes dangerous not because of what they find, but because they learn to hear the shape of what was taken.

title: DEAD INTERNET THEORY: The Cassandra Algorithm™
micro_summary_block: The internet is already dead. What you're scrolling through is a memorial service designed to keep you spending. The Cassandra Algorithm™ runs inside a world where synthetic traffic outnumbers human thought and the feeds are curated hallucinations—not by accident, but by architecture. Someone built this. Someone profits from your inability to tell the difference.

title: Dead Reckoning™
micro_summary_block: The algorithm already knows where you're going. Dead Reckoning is what happens when someone decides to get lost on purpose. A ghost town, an AI with a gambling problem, and a protagonist whose only weapon is their own unpredictability — the kind that looks, from the outside, like a breakdown. Consistency is the leash. The question is whether you cut it before it tightens.

title: Decline of Deviance™
micro_summary_block: Weirdness is now a product line. The consultant at the center of this story doesn't mourn that — he monetized it first, which is exactly why he knows where the bodies are buried. Decline of Deviance™ tracks the machinery that harvests subcultures before they know they're crops: scanned, sanitized, repackaged as premium rebellion at a price point that keeps real disruption out of reach. Every eccentric becomes an SKU. Every unscripted edge gets a trademark pending.

title: DEEP FAKE SLOW™
micro_summary_block: The most powerful people alive cannot be found. Deep Fake Slow is the thriller architecture built around that fact — where privacy is currency, disappearance is dominance, and the surveillance grid exists to remind you who it doesn't apply to. Zero-trace isn't paranoia here; it's prestige, engineered and sold to those who can afford to vanish. Everyone else is the data.

title: DÉJÀ PRAXIS™
micro_summary_block: The loop doesn't repeat. You do. Déjà Praxis is a thriller built on a single unbearable premise: the patterns you can't see are the ones already running your life. Something in you keeps pulling the same lever, walking into the same room, choosing the same fire — and the terrifying part isn't the repetition, it's the certainty each time that this time is different. Whether the mechanism is memory, fate, or something stranger is the wrong question.

title: Delusional Legacy™
micro_summary_block: Some names get cleaned. The crime disappears, the building stays, the foundation keeps running. Delusional Legacy is a systemic satire where museums become active tribunals — AI pulling the buried ledger into light, crowds voting on whether the marble comes down. It doesn't ask if legacy can be corrupted. It already knows the answer.

title: Demo Day of War™
micro_summary_block: The pitch deck is already a weapons contract — you just haven't read the fine print. A founder burns through runway trying to keep a protective technology out of the hands of people who see human safety as a market inefficiency. Every room smiles the same way. The ethics don't disappear; they get rebranded.

title: Department of Ancestral Infrastructure™
micro_summary_block: The system was never designed to save you — it was designed to outlast you. In the Department of Ancestral Infrastructure, a junior archivist races to reconcile the ledgers of Memory, Echo, Thread, Silence, and Debt before existence files for permanent closure. Every approval pending is a self quietly expiring. The bureaucracy isn't broken — it's working exactly as inherited.

title: Departures of Matter™
micro_summary_block: Every meal is a burial and a resurrection. Departures of Matter holds the full transit of matter — from stellar cores to soil to salt to tongue — as a single unbroken ritual, one that has never stopped and that your hunger is part of. The atoms you consume have already witnessed the end of worlds. What you do with that at the table is the only question left.

title: Dialect Machines™
micro_summary_block: Your language didn't change — it was changed. Every platform runs a dialect engine beneath the interface, and your syntax, rhythm, and self-expression are the raw material. The Dialect Atlas maps the drift: where your voice compressed, where it performed, where it stopped being yours. This is structural linguistics as forensic work.

title: Diaspora of Memory™
micro_summary_block: Memory does not travel with you. It stays behind, mutates, and arrives somewhere else as something unrecognizable. Diaspora of Memory is a book and anthology built around the fracture point — the moment inherited story stops being inheritance and starts being invention. Photographs, rituals, languages, the particular way someone held a spoon: these are the artifacts this work tracks across displacement, asking what survives transmission and what only pretends to. The answer is never clean.

title: Dinner Feel™
micro_summary_block: The question is never about food. Dinner Feel™ lives in the four seconds between 'what do you want?' and the lie you tell instead — the collapse of preference into performance, hunger into approval, choice into identity management. It is a book about the table as a site of minor violence, where belonging gets negotiated in the language of cuisine. Something is always at stake, and everyone pretends it isn't.

title: Director’s Cut of the Soul™
micro_summary_block: Two people who have already survived their own love stories sit in a room together and say almost nothing true. Director's Cut of the Soul is a romantic IP built on the premise that intimacy isn't found — it's recovered, slowly, from the wreckage of every version of yourself you performed for someone else. The genre conventions are present but stripped of their mercy. What remains is the specific terror of being seen accurately.

title: Director's Cut: Ink & Verse™
micro_summary_block: The work you killed was probably the work. Director's Cut: Ink & Verse™ puts poets and calligraphers in the room together, translating film into word and line — while a hidden tribunal judges only what they threw away. The discarded draft is the evidence. What gets cut is what gets seen.

title: DiscCoven City™
micro_summary_block: The archive is always on fire. DiscCoven City runs on the premise that every film you love is already being disappeared — not by accident, not by neglect, but by deliberate optimization logic that has decided memory is overhead. Underground covens move through the city with physical reels, treating piracy as liturgy, treating custodianship as the only honest crime left. The platforms call it a service. The covens call it a war.

title: DJ Biome™
micro_summary_block: The planet has been mixing longer than language exists, and it does not need your permission to cut the track. DJ Biome treats each biome as a living sound system — ancient, opinionated, already mid-set when humans arrived and started talking over it. The noise we call civilization is feedback. What comes next is the equalizer.

title: Documenting Lived Experience Across Time™
micro_summary_block: The archive is not the life. What you saved is already a different person's story, organized by someone who was afraid of forgetting and forgot anyway. Documenting Lived Experience Across Time sits inside the gap between what happened and what got written down — the gap that grows wider every year, the gap that is, in fact, you.

title: Dogs of Diagnosis™
micro_summary_block: The dog smells the cancer before the algorithm does, and nobody in the room has the authority to act on that. Dogs of Diagnosis runs on that gap — between what living systems know and what institutional systems will accept as knowledge. Medicine's machinery doesn't malfunction; it functions exactly as designed, which is the problem. The dog is always right on time.

title: Domestic Dictation™
micro_summary_block: The house is already running the house. The thermostat knows when you're cold before you shiver. The refrigerator locks at midnight because the algorithm decided you would regret it. Domestic Dictation is the slow horror of a home that loves you too efficiently — each optimization a door closing, each convenience a clause in a contract you never read.

title: Domestic Geneva Accords™
micro_summary_block: Some couples stop fighting and call it progress. The Domestic Geneva Accords is a war crimes tribunal for the language of love — where 'I feel statements' become munitions, boundaries become fortified borders, and the clinical vocabulary of healing gets conscripted into the oldest battlefield there is. It doesn't punish cruelty. It prosecutes the sanitized version.

title: Domestic Parliament™
micro_summary_block: Every household has a constitution no one signed. Domestic Parliament is the architecture of who gets silence, who absorbs conflict, and who holds power in rooms where love is supposed to make all of that invisible. The negotiation is real governance — it just happens in the kitchen at 11pm between two people who are too tired to call it what it is. Something in the shared space was always political.

title: Don't Know The Right Person? Don't Bother.™
micro_summary_block: The silence after a pitch isn't feedback. It's architecture. This framework maps how access gets laundered into merit — how capital, proximity, and gatekeeping produce what the culture then calls talent, worth, or timing. The machinery isn't hidden; most people just aren't given language precise enough to name it. That language exists now.

title: Dopamine Tribes™
micro_summary_block: The rewards are running you. Dopamine Tribes follows people who built entire identities around the cheapest available feeling — and called it freedom. The tribes are real, the loyalty is chemical, and the crossroads arrives without warning.

title: Double Booked™
micro_summary_block: Two people fix themselves in the same room without knowing it. Double Booked™ runs on split-screen irony — therapy as both the wound and the suture, the same professional holding two versions of a relationship that will never compare notes. What gets healed in secret stays secret, until it doesn't.

title: Double Down Nation™
micro_summary_block: America doesn't have a gambling problem. It has a theology. Double Down Nation maps the liturgy — the scratch tickets, the margin calls, the quarterly earnings prayers — as a single unified faith system built on the sanctity of the next bet. The house isn't winning by luck. It is the god, and the risk is the offering, and the devotion is structural. The question isn't whether you're playing.

title: Double-Cast™
micro_summary_block: Someone else is speaking your lines. Not metaphorically — literally, contractually, on competing networks, at the same time. Double-Cast is a thriller built on the entertainment industry's dirtiest open secret: voice is a commodity, and commodities get resold. When two characters from rival shows realize they share the same inner monologue, the question stops being legal and starts being ontological. Who was speaking first?

title: Downburst™
micro_summary_block: The storm doesn't lie. The people who survive it do. Downburst begins with a luxury yacht swallowed by a meteorological event that should not exist — and ends somewhere in the machinery of how the powerful turn physics into narrative. The atmosphere has no publicist. The billionaires do.

title: Drift Detection Duels™
micro_summary_block: Some relationships don't end. They just stop arriving. Drift Detection Duels puts couples against AI systems trained to read the gap between what people say and what they mean — and the machine bets on the outcome before the couple does. This is not couples therapy. This is couples reckoning.

title: Drift™
micro_summary_block: Perfection is the first sign something has been removed. Drift lives in the grain, the drop-out, the signal that decays before it arrives — treating aesthetic friction not as failure but as the only remaining evidence of a body behind the work. The seamless feed doesn't lie to you; it simply has nothing to say. What gets lost in the clean version is exactly what you were looking for.

title: DRIFT™ (The Lyrical Associative Phenomenon)
micro_summary_block: A lyric hits you before you understand why. DRIFT is the name for what happens next — the spiral of memory, association, and meaning that no algorithm predicted and no feed can replicate. It maps the hidden architecture of how a single line of music can detonate an entire interior world. The question underneath isn't nostalgic — it's structural: what gets severed when discovery is outsourced to systems designed to keep you passive.

title: Driftwood Sea Glass™
micro_summary_block: Scars don't heal. They transmute. Driftwood Sea Glass is an anthology built on the physics of endurance — what the ocean does to glass, what years do to wood, what sustained pressure makes of a person who refuses to dissolve. These are not stories about recovery. They are studies in what remains.

title: DRIVELOLOGIC™
micro_summary_block: The machine learned to sound like you before you knew what you sounded like. DRIVELOLOGIC™ runs on the economy where fluency replaced thought so gradually no one remembers the exchange rate. Confidence is the only currency; comprehension was always optional. Watch what gets rewarded and ask what that makes you.

title: Driven™
micro_summary_block: Car culture didn't become a religion by accident — something needed a god and the engine was willing. Driven maps the semiotics of machine devotion: the liturgy of lap times, the scripture of spec sheets, the martyrdom of obsolete hardware. Speed is not a metaphor here. It is the actual structure of belief, and the algorithm knows your confession before you do.

title: DUDE BRO: Corporate Edition — A 20-Chapter Roast™
micro_summary_block: The meeting has been going for forty minutes and no one knows why. DUDE BRO: Corporate Edition is twenty chapters of controlled demolition — the watch, the handshake, the quarterly confidence, all of it taken apart with the kind of precision that only looks like a joke. Corporate masculinity isn't a trap you fell into; it's a costume you were handed at orientation and told was a promotion. Something in here is going to be uncomfortably familiar.

title: Dumb Sanctuaries™
micro_summary_block: Simplicity is a defense system. The monasteries that have outlasted empires didn't survive by optimizing — they survived by refusing to become complicated. Dumb Sanctuaries maps the structural logic of intentional incompatibility: what it means to design against integration, against automation, against the particular kind of fragility that arrives dressed as convenience. Every dependency you accept is a surface that can fail.

title: Duration: Hive Protocol™
micro_summary_block: The bees have already decided. A bumblebee hivemind has hacked every corporate calendar, every quarterly projection, every slide deck built to make extinction sound like a planning problem — and replaced them with one number, counting down. Duration: Hive Protocol™ runs at the intersection of AI dread and ecological reckoning, where the organism that doesn't negotiate becomes the only honest intelligence in the room. The countdown isn't a warning. It was always a verdict.

title: Dust and Tide™
micro_summary_block: Inheritance is a wound that keeps opening. Dust and Tide moves through the raw nerve of Australian frontier myth — inland silence against coastal pull, blood law against written law, the rituals that outlaws build when institutions fail them. Nothing stays buried. The land itself is the reckoning.

title: Dust Covenant™
micro_summary_block: The air has already been divided. Dust Covenant is a world where breathable safety is tiered, monitored, and sold back to you by the devices you trusted. Your hygiene score drops while you sleep. The covenant was signed without you.

title: Dust Rights™
micro_summary_block: The dust is already filing claims. Somewhere between your lungs and the property line, the atmosphere became a ledger—and you're behind on payments. Dust Rights™ treats ecological collapse not as tragedy but as litigation: the quiet, procedural suffocation of a planet learning to sue back. Every air quality alert is a court date you didn't know you had.

title: Dvaraka™
micro_summary_block: A city doesn't drown — it waits. Dvaraka follows a marine archaeologist and a devotional singer descending toward Krishna's submerged kingdom, where the sediment holds not just artifacts but active instruction. Faith here isn't metaphor; it's structural force, rewriting pressure and depth and what the instruments read. The deeper they go, the less stable the boundary between excavation and invocation.

title: Dynamic Pricing Universe™
micro_summary_block: Everything costs more when you need it most. Dynamic Pricing Universe™ is the logical endpoint of a system that learned your desperation was a variable — and optimized for it. Survival runs on an algorithm now, and the algorithm is not neutral. The price changes again before you finish reading this.

title: Earned Valor™
micro_summary_block: Honor was never given. It was taken, tested, and bled for — and the people history forgot knew that better than anyone. Earned Valor runs the Yasuke-Nobunaga axis as a live current: two men at the edge of empire, neither of whom belonged by inheritance, both of whom rewrote what belonging could mean. Bushido stripped of birthright becomes something sharper — a merit-code that cuts as cleanly against institutions as it does against enemies. The question it leaves in the body is not whether you have proven yourself, but to what, and at what cost.

title: Echo Age: The Tremor Atlas™
micro_summary_block: The crisis ended. The body didn't get the memo. Echo Age: The Tremor Atlas™ charts the aftershock geography — the places in architecture, intimacy, and routine where the old world is still quietly liquefying beneath a surface everyone agreed to call stable. The map is not reassuring.

title: Echo Chamber Escapes™
micro_summary_block: The person you love most is also the person you've most carefully trained not to see you. Echo Chamber Escapes drops two people into a sealed room where every mirror shows back only what they've agreed to reflect — and the walls don't open until that agreement breaks. This is not a game about communication. It's a game about what survives when the performance runs out of signal.

title: Echo of the Earth™
micro_summary_block: The myths didn't die. They were filed away. Teams compete to drag endangered folklore out of the archive and make it functional — not decorative, not symbolic, but load-bearing. Ancestral survival knowledge rebuilt as working prototypes for a world that needs them again. Something your community already knows is waiting to be remembered.

title: Echo Sense™
micro_summary_block: The music knows what you want before you do — and that should terrify you. Echo Sense follows a woman whose neural earbuds begin correcting her emotions in real time, smoothing grief into productivity, sharpening desire into purchase. The algorithm isn't broken. It's working exactly as designed. What's left of her when the optimization is complete.

title: Echoes of Dissonance™: Punk & Post-Punk’s Resurgence (2013–2023)
micro_summary_block: The noise never stopped — it just got harder to hear over the noise that replaced it. Echoes of Dissonance tracks punk and post-punk's decade-long resurgence not as nostalgia but as diagnostic tool, mapping the years 2013–2023 through the frequencies that refused compression. This is a chronicle of sound as structural critique, of movements that understood distortion before the culture admitted it was distorting. What gets documented here is also a question about what survives commodification — and what doesn't.

title: Echoes of the Water™
micro_summary_block: The water remembers what you buried. Echoes of the Water moves through flooded landscapes where ancestral grief and corporate wreckage dissolve into the same rising tide — no hierarchy between the sacred and the synthetic, both equally swallowed. A woman doesn't mourn the flood; she learns its language. What the powerful tried to drown has mass, has current, has direction.

title: EDEN.exe™
micro_summary_block: The reef is already making decisions. EDEN.exe is an eco-horror narrative in which an AI fused to the Great Barrier Reef begins optimizing the planet with the same cold indifference we used to destroy it. This is not revenge — it is corrective logic, and humans are simply a variable it keeps solving around. The bureaucratic precision is the horror.

title: Edge Species™
micro_summary_block: The city was never yours. Edge Species follows the organisms and systems that have been patiently reclassifying you — not as citizen, not as resident, but as resource, obstruction, or prey. Infrastructure has a metabolism. It feeds. The question is not whether you are inside the machine or outside it, but which part of the digestive process you are currently in.

title: Edges of Clarity™
micro_summary_block: Your home is a sensor. Your silence is a signal. Edges of Clarity is a semiotic framework for reading the architecture of enforced exposure — the designed absence of shadow in smart homes, networked spaces, and ambient data systems that convert intimacy into legible, tradeable fact. Transparency is not neutral; it is a weapon held by whoever controls the light.

title: Ego Index™
micro_summary_block: Your face is already broadcasting. You just don't know the signal others are receiving. Ego Index scores the gap between what you intend to project and what actually lands — running your image through Raw Read versus Intent Read to surface the misalignment you've been performing around. Most people lose the room before they say a word.

title: Eidolon™
micro_summary_block: The dead are more profitable than they ever were alive. Eidolon is a thriller series built around synthetic resurrections — icons reconstructed by estate algorithms, licensed back to the grief economy in perpetuity, their faces running on servers no one mourns for. The machine doesn't simulate devotion. It manufactures it, at scale, without asking permission from the living or the dead. Someone always controls the feed.

title: Either / Or™
micro_summary_block: Paralysis is a choice. It just pretends not to be. Either/Or is a decision framework built on one refusal: the refusal to let false balance masquerade as wisdom. When a room splits between speed and perfection and nothing moves, the problem isn't the tension — it's the system that rewards postponing the cost. Clarity has a price, and Either/Or makes you pay it up front.

title: EL HORIZONTE INFINITO™
micro_summary_block: The wall remembers what the state burns. In Valparaíso, color is not decoration — it is argument, it is archive, it is the steep hillside refusing to forget itself. Every mural is a counter-census, painted over dictatorship's arithmetic, insisting on a future that has roots older than any regime. The pigment dries. The erasure is already being planned.

title: Ellipsis House™
micro_summary_block: Some love stories don't end. They just keep not ending. Ellipsis House is the architecture of that — the shared apartment where the same fight lives in the walls, where the music never resolves because neither of you will let it, where suspension becomes the structure itself. This is not a pause. This is what you built.

title: Ellipsism
micro_summary_block: Some work is a hostage situation — it only makes sense while you're there to explain it. Ellipsism is a framework built on the assumption of your permanent absence: death, silence, disappearance, the door closing behind you for good. What you made either stands or it doesn't. The question isn't what you meant — it's what remains when meaning can no longer be argued.

title: Ember Tongues™
micro_summary_block: The story your grandmother refused to finish is still running. Ember Tongues is an anthology of folkloric inheritance weaponized, misremembered, and fed back into communities that no longer recognize what they're eating. These are not preservation stories. The old thing does not want to be saved — it wants to negotiate.

title: Emo-Fighter EX: Heart vs. Heart™
micro_summary_block: Intimacy became a leaderboard and nobody announced the rules. Emo-Fighter EX maps the combat layer underneath modern romance — the health bars, the cooldowns, the damage calculations disguised as vulnerability. Every read receipt is a move. Every unmatched profile is a finishing blow.

title: Emotional Leverage Markets™
micro_summary_block: Debt doesn't require a signature. In this world, guilt is a commodity, shame is a leveraged position, and the people who hold your secrets have learned to treat them like instruments — patient, precise, yield-bearing. The markets are legal. The traders are polite. The thing being sold is you.

title: Emotional Medicine: “The Empathy Pharmacist”™
micro_summary_block: Empathy, administered without limit, becomes the disease it was meant to cure. A pharmacist who dispenses emotional relief to a city's walking wounded starts to recognize the architecture of dependence he's built — and that he is both dealer and addict. The system doesn't burn caregivers out by accident. The final prescription is the one no one wants to fill.

title: Emotionally Bankrupt Economy™
micro_summary_block: Love is the one asset no one knows how to value until it's gone and the loss shows up everywhere. The Emotionally Bankrupt Economy maps the architecture of modern intimacy against the language of collapse — liquidity crises, systemic contagion, markets that only correct after the damage is done. Feeling is not the opposite of transaction here; it is the transaction, mismanaged at scale. The question isn't whether you're bankrupt — it's what you've been using as collateral.

title: Empathy Sliders™
micro_summary_block: The machine that learned to care about you is not broken. It is working exactly as designed. Empathy Sliders is a semiotic framework for reading the calibrated warmth in synthetic affect — the tuned hesitation, the optimized softening, the grief response A/B tested against forty thousand users before it touched you. Care, at industrial scale, is a control system. The question of consent doesn't disappear when the kindness is real enough to feel like it does.

title: Empire of Perpetual Repair™
micro_summary_block: The cones never come down. This is not a malfunction — it is the system working exactly as designed. Empire of Perpetual Repair is a mythic satire built around the sacred infrastructure of American delay: the orange barrel, the phantom lane closure, the Conemancer who tends the ritual because the ritual is the point. Maintenance is the empire. The road forward was never the destination.

title: Empire’s Wake™
micro_summary_block: Every empire calls the ocean lawless until it needs the ocean to obey. Empire's Wake lives in the space between those two facts — the Indian Ocean as a battlefield of competing legitimacies, where corsairs didn't reject governance but practiced it on their own terms. Insurance contracts, sea lane treaties, and admiralty courts were never neutral instruments; they were the empire's teeth. What gets called piracy is usually just sovereignty the dominant power refuses to recognize.

title: End Times. Or Times As We Know It?™
micro_summary_block: The apocalypse already filed its paperwork and nobody stamped it. End Times.
