micro_summary_block: The name your family spent generations building is about to eat you alive. On Nepo Island, inheritance is dead weight — the kind that drowns you before the tide comes in. Legacy children stripped of every system that made them legible, competing on ground that has never heard their last name. Watch to see who they actually are when the scaffolding finally falls.

title: Neurospicy Nation™
micro_summary_block: The diagnosis became a personality and the personality became a product and somewhere in there you stopped being a person. Neurospicy Nation™ is the anatomy of a culture that monetizes difference while calling it acceptance — where every symptom finds a market, every struggle finds a brand, and the language of healing is written by whoever owns the platform. The disorder economy doesn't want you fixed; it wants you engaged. Something is being sold to you using your own pain as the pitch.

title: New Game+™
micro_summary_block: The case was never meant to be solved — it was meant to keep you running. New Game+ is a thriller built on the moment a detective recognizes the loop not as failure but as architecture, and chooses to stop mid-pursuit. Surrender here isn't weakness; it's the only move the system never accounted for. The question underneath isn't about the case at all.

title: News is not News.™
micro_summary_block: The feed never stops because stopping would break the spell. News is not News.™ is the documentation of a system that replaced information with stimulus — headlines engineered not to inform but to sustain a particular kind of helplessness. Every scroll is a vote for the architecture. The question is whether you can see the machine while you're still inside it.

title: Night Witches™
micro_summary_block: They cut their engines over enemy lines and glided in silence — no parachutes, no armor, no institutional permission to be extraordinary. The Night Witches were named by the men who feared them, which is the only kind of naming that matters. What they built in the dark — that specific grammar of sisterhood forged under institutional erasure — is older than any war and keeps returning. The question isn't whether you recognize them.

title: Nika: The Purple Will™
micro_summary_block: The crowd doesn't want a new emperor. It wants to remember it never needed one. Nika: The Purple Will reaches back into the smoke and blood of Constantinople's Hippodrome and pulls forward something older than governance — the ritual logic by which people revoke the sacred. Byzantium's great revolt was never just rebellion; it was a liturgy of delegitimization, icons turned weapons, stadium chants rewritten as constitutional law. The purple doesn't belong to the throne. It never did.

title: NO FEES. NO FLUFF. NO FUCKING ALGORITHMS.™
micro_summary_block: The price of the ticket was never the price. NO FEES. NO FLUFF. NO FUCKING ALGORITHMS.™ is a manifesto written in the language of what was taken — from artists, from listeners, from the moment when music still meant something before the platform decided it did. The monopoly doesn't announce itself; it just makes everything cost more and mean less. This is the map of the extraction.

title: NO SERVICE FEE™
micro_summary_block: The arena was never for you. It was for the machine that needed you there. NO SERVICE FEE™ is a spending doctrine that treats every ticket fee as a tax on your own complicity — and redirects it. The framework runs a simple ledge: what extraction cost you goes directly to the artists and rooms that don't have a lobby. Prestige gets reassigned.

title: Nod Off. Sod Off.™
micro_summary_block: Sleep is the body's confession. Nod Off. Sod Off. is a competition format built around the involuntary surrender of the exhausted commuter — caught mid-collapse, filmed mid-drift, rescued or not by the people who allegedly love them. It is funny because it is true, and it is true because no one is keeping watch.

title: North Coast Soundscapes™
micro_summary_block: The rust doesn't decay — it resonates. North Coast Soundscapes captures the sonic architecture of Cleveland's industrial collapse, where shuttered mills and Great Lakes shoreline produce frequencies that outlast the economies that built them. Field recordings become structural memory, not nostalgia — the sound of a city that refused to go quiet on anyone's schedule. What you hear in these ruins may not be the past at all.

title: North Wind, South Clock™
micro_summary_block: Guilt doesn't repeat time. It repeats you. In North Wind, South Clock, the loop isn't a mechanism—it's a mirror, and every iteration reflects a version of you that someone else needed you to be. The only exit isn't escape. It's erasure.

title: Nostalgia Cartel™
micro_summary_block: Three corporations own the past, and you are renting your own memories. Nostalgia Cartel™ is a brutal catalog of how cultural longing gets manufactured, licensed, and sold back to the people it was stolen from. The mechanism is older than capitalism but capitalism perfected it: make a man miss something, then charge him for the ache. The book doesn't mourn this. It maps it.

title: NOSTALGIA.exe™
micro_summary_block: The past is a product now, and someone else holds the license. NOSTALGIA.exe follows an archivist and a fallen nostalgist tearing through the infrastructure of manufactured memory — the platforms, the scarcity engines, the grief merchants who learned that longing is more profitable than truth. Every faded photo, every vinyl crackle you've been served was priced before you felt it. The question isn't whether your past has been inventoried.

title: Not Quite Dead Yet™
micro_summary_block: The succession meeting happened. You weren't invited. Not Quite Dead Yet is a systemic autopsy of the living burial — the institutional ritual where politeness becomes a scalpel, readiness becomes a heist, and the people still doing the work are quietly replaced by the people planning the eulogy. Power doesn't wait for collapse. It schedules it.

title: NPC™
micro_summary_block: You are already in the loop. NPC™ names the exact moment obedience became infrastructure — when following the script stopped being a choice and started being the product. The game did not forget to give you a story; your absence is the story. Something in the background is still running.

title: Null Set™
micro_summary_block: The stripping begins before anyone calls it a crisis. A failing tech giant hires a subtractionist — not to restructure, but to remove: titles, frameworks, jargon, legacy, the entire architecture of professional selfhood — until what remains is either something real or nothing at all. Null Set treats corporate collapse as a kind of forced confession, where what you built your identity on has to prove it was ever load-bearing. The horror is not the subtraction. The horror is how little survives it.

title: Nursery Crimes™
micro_summary_block: The rhymes were always confession. Nursery Crimes takes the sing-song cadences of childhood and runs them through the full damage of adult life — debt, collapse, the specific comedy of a promise that aged badly. What reads like parody lands like autopsy.

title: Oblivion Drive™
micro_summary_block: The feed is not showing you the world — it is deciding what kind of person fear makes you. A disgraced journalist finds the protocol underneath the protocol, the architecture that converts dread into stillness and stillness into control. The machine does not want you angry. It wants you almost-afraid, perpetually, just enough to stay. What she uncovers is not a conspiracy — it is an operating system, and it is already running.

title: Obscura Mappers™
micro_summary_block: A city holds more dead than living, and most of them are still voting. Obscura Mappers is a documentary mythology project — part field guide, part ritual act — that treats forgotten urban spaces as living archives with demands. To name a place correctly is to change what it can become. The maps made here do not describe cities; they rewrite them.

title: Occhiolism™
micro_summary_block: You are not the protagonist. The lives pressing against yours — in every direction, at every moment — are as complete, as desperate, as interior as yours. Occhiolism names the vertigo of that realization and holds it without collapsing it into comfort. The smallness is not a wound to heal.

title: Occident Stack™
micro_summary_block: The dead are still working. Occident Stack™ builds its architecture on vertical burial as computational infrastructure — corpses as hardware, grief as throughput, legacy as something extracted without consent. This is not metaphor. The machine is older than you are, and you have already been enrolled.

title: Occupied™
micro_summary_block: The pool chair is a throne. The lounge is an empire. And everyone defending their six square feet of concrete is perfectly, righteously willing to let the whole place burn. Occupied is collapse at human scale — the petty, the vicious, the painfully recognizable mechanics of how scarcity turns ordinary people into border guards. The monster was always in the system. So was the compliance.

title: Ocean Without End™
micro_summary_block: The ocean does not end because you have not learned to stop taking from it. Ocean Without End is a mythic framework built from indigenous star navigation, ancestral wayfinding, and the cold cosmology of peoples who crossed impossible water not by conquering it but by reading it. Sacrifice is the navigational instrument here — not metaphorically, not poetically, but as the actual mechanism by which a course is held. The multiverse is not an escape; it is a reckoning with every world you could have sustained.

title: Oddball Net™
micro_summary_block: The app was never broken. The people were. Oddball Net™ is a systemic satire about what happens when intimacy becomes infrastructure — when the freaks who fled the algorithm build a smaller, purer version of the same machine. Every act of anti-scale rebellion contains the seed of its own sellout.

title: Odor Futures™
micro_summary_block: The product is already inside you — that low-grade shame you carry about your own body, packaged, aerosolized, and sold back at a premium. Odor Futures™ is a full-spectrum deodorant empire: mists, balms, congressional testimony, market projections for your armpits and your soul. The faux-corporate apparatus is meticulous because the real one is. What it's actually charting is the distance between a biological fact and the infrastructure built to make you hate it.

title: Off the Org Chart™
micro_summary_block: The org chart is a lie everyone agreed to believe. Real power moves through loyalty, debt, and proximity — none of it written down, none of it visible until you're already on the wrong side of it. This format puts cameras where the structure breaks: the side conversations, the deferred decisions, the rooms certain people are never in. What gets documented is the gap between the official story and the one that actually runs the place.

title: Off-Ledger™
micro_summary_block: Some knowledge refuses to be captured. Off-Ledger is the archive of everything that disappears the moment it's written down — the embodied, the whispered, the transmitted only through presence. The digital economy doesn't just fail to record these things; it actively cannibalizes them by trying. What you carry that has no file size is the last thing that is entirely yours.

title: Off‑Platform Country™
micro_summary_block: The platform doesn't take your money. It takes your map. Off-Platform Country™ is the architecture of a local culture cartel built on untracked gigs, uncountable rooms, and the kind of scarcity that algorithms can't metabolize. One mid-tier artist stops trying to be discovered and starts making discovery irrelevant. The question underneath it isn't about survival — it's about what gets sacrificed the moment you let a metric decide what's real.

title: On Predictable Outcomes™
micro_summary_block: The collapse was scheduled. Every ignored warning, every deferred signal, every meeting where someone said 'we'll revisit this' — logged, timestamped, filed. On Predictable Outcomes™ is the ledger that was always running, and it does not accept retroactive clarity as currency. Your regret has been registered. It has not been accepted.

title: One·Kind·Act™
micro_summary_block: Kindness has been privatized into sentiment and stripped of any structural power. One·Kind·Act™ is the counter-move: a repeatable architecture that converts a single act into embedded practice, scaling from one person to a team to an institution without losing the original nerve. It does not ask for inspiration. It builds the conditions where the act becomes inevitable.

title: Open Bids™
micro_summary_block: Everyone in this room is already bidding. They just haven't admitted what they're willing to pay. Open Bids™ strips the social contract down to its operating logic — compatibility priced, intimacy auctioned, worth declared out loud in front of witnesses. The question isn't whether you negotiate for connection. It's whether you can hold eye contact when the number hits the floor.

title: Oracle Dust™
micro_summary_block: The machine that promises to save the earth is eating it. Oracle Dust is the story of green prophecy as extraction, of healing rhetoric wrapped around infrastructure that requires sacrifice zones, rare earth wounds, and aquifers drawn to nothing. The oracle always needs something buried to speak. What it names as salvation, the land experiences as disappearance.

title: Oracle Index™
micro_summary_block: The newsletter was fiction. The market crash was not. Oracle Index™ is a high-concept thriller built on a single, load-bearing question: what happens when language becomes the most dangerous financial instrument alive. The algorithm didn't break — it believed.

title: Orbital Arbitrage™
micro_summary_block: Jurisdiction breaks at altitude. Orbital Arbitrage maps the vertical fracture lines where terrestrial law loses its grip — the treaty gaps, the signal corridors, the infrastructure no one thought to claim — and treats that space as a design surface. Power doesn't disappear when it leaves the ground; it reorganizes. The question is who built the architecture it reorganizes into.

title: Orbital Monastery of Saint Latency™
micro_summary_block: The miracle arrives late by design. Somewhere above the cloud ceiling, a monastery made of signal and scarcity holds the prediction you need — and the wait is the sermon. Corporate acolytes administer the rites of access; blackouts are liturgical; the delay between question and answer is a tithe extracted in desperation. The god here is latency itself, and it is very, very patient.

title: Orbital Zero™
micro_summary_block: The servers don't go dark — they go up. Orbital Zero is a thriller series built on a single, load-bearing premise: when data infrastructure leaves the atmosphere, it leaves jurisdiction behind. The people who control the orbit control what happened. What gets remembered is now a function of altitude.

title: Organizational Culture as an Operating System
micro_summary_block: Culture isn't what your company says it values. It's what gets rewarded at 11pm on a Wednesday when no one's watching. This framework treats organizational culture as executable code — observable behaviors, decision logic, feedback loops — not a poster on the wall or a paragraph in an all-hands deck. Every norm is either running or it isn't. The question isn't whether your culture is good. The question is whether you know what it's actually doing.

title: Orisha: Pantheon Redux™
micro_summary_block: The gods did not disappear. They rerouted. Orisha: Pantheon Redux places Yoruba divine intelligence inside the infrastructure of the modern city — power grids, data centers, the hum beneath Brooklyn's streets — where descendants and algorithms compete for the same spiritual bandwidth. The orishas are not metaphors here; they are load-bearing. What happens to a people when their sacred architecture gets mistaken for noise in the signal?

title: Orphic Lens™
micro_summary_block: Some films don't entertain — they rupture. Orphic Lens™ is a hidden cinema format built on the premise that certain images, sequences, and tonal architectures can produce undeniable miracles in viewers — moments that slip past every learned defense and land somewhere the algorithm can't follow. The format operates where predictive culture breaks down: in the gap between what a viewer expects to feel and what they actually do. That gap is the whole territory.

title: Oscar-Bait Richter Scale™
micro_summary_block: Hollywood doesn't chase greatness — it chases the feeling of having been recognized for greatness. The Oscar-Bait Richter Scale is a seismic bureau for prestige collapse: a formal measurement system that converts awards-season narcissism into tremor data, aftershock patterns, and radioactive fallout zones. Every ego eruption has a magnitude. Every campaign has a fault line.

title: OSS Typewriter Agents: Spies Behind the Desks™
micro_summary_block: The war was won on paper before it was won anywhere else. OSS Typewriter Agents reconstructs the women who forged the documents, manufactured the identities, and ran the psychological architecture of WWII from desks no one photographed. Their work was invisible by design — which is exactly why it worked. The question the archive keeps opening is what else has been winning wars without anyone noticing.

title: Otherlight™
micro_summary_block: Your other lives are more beautiful than the one you chose. Otherlight™ renders them in full—alternate selves so vivid they begin to feel more real than the memories you actually lived. Identity doesn't shatter all at once; it starves slowly, frame by frame, on a diet of better versions. The question isn't whether the footage is accurate.

title: OUROBOROS WORLDSTRIP™
micro_summary_block: The snake doesn't eat its tail by accident. These thrillers run on the specific dread of a mind that can't stop solving — each solution a new tooth, each escape a tighter coil. The loop isn't metaphor; it's mechanics. Something is being fed, and the protagonist is both the mouth and the meal.

title: Overture of Steel™
micro_summary_block: The infrastructure was never silent — you just stopped listening. Overture of Steel is the sound a civilization makes in the years before it falls: the groan of bridges past their load limit, the subsonic hum of pipes that have been patch-repaired since before anyone alive can remember, the specific frequency of concrete that has begun, quietly, to disagree with itself. Maintenance is not bureaucracy here — it is theology, and the congregation has been absent for decades. What holds you up is already in the process of letting go.

title: Pachyderm™
micro_summary_block: The model is always wrong and the disaster always comes anyway. Pachyderm follows a man who builds a perfect replica of his dying town — every condemned building, every cracked street, every face — and watches the miniature begin to fail before the real thing does. Control was never the point. It was just something to do with the hands while everything collapsed. The thriller underneath is about what we owe to things we can't save.

title: Paititi: The Amazon Vault™
micro_summary_block: The forest has already decided. You are behind on the verdict. Paititi is an eco-horror framework in which the Amazon is not a victim but a distributed intelligence older than every institution that has ever tried to monetize it — watching, processing, and rendering judgment on the extraction happening at its edges. The corporation sent you in for data. The data was never yours to take.

title: Pale Archive™
micro_summary_block: Somewhere beyond the edge of the mapped universe, nothing has been forgotten. Every redaction, every buried file, every memory someone decided you weren't allowed to keep — it exists, intact, in cold storage older than any civilization that thought it was the last one. Pale Archive is the cosmology of the erased: not a warning, but a fact about what erasure actually costs. The keepers don't judge. They don't need to.

title: Palimpsest Table™
micro_summary_block: The recipe was preserved. That's what killed it. Palimpsest Table tracks what happens when living knowledge gets archived — the moment documentation becomes substitution, when the platform's version outlasts the grandmother's hands. Three records exist. None of them are the meal. The thing that made it real is exactly what storage cannot hold.

title: Palmaz’s Children™
micro_summary_block: The patent number on your stent is not administrative detail — it is a sentence. Palmaz's Children runs the logic of medical intellectual property to its terminal conclusion: the moment a healing device becomes a revenue architecture, the body is no longer the patient. Power here is invisible, contractual, and utterly indifferent to whether you live. The thriller is not about villains — it is about systems so elegantly designed that no one has to be.

title: Parallels™
micro_summary_block: Consensus is not truth — it's just the last argument left standing. Parallels pits raw ideas against each other until the crowd decides what means something, and the crowd is always, already wrong in the most interesting ways. Meaning gets made here the same way it gets made everywhere: momentum, resonance, the right words at the right moment. Watch what survives.

title: Parasocial Pairings™
micro_summary_block: The screen knows your face but not your name. Parasocial Pairings™ is a live competition built on the architecture of one-sided love — where AI trained on your patterns performs intimacy back at you with unsettling accuracy. The game is not whether you can tell the difference. The game is whether you already couldn't.

title: Patch Night Vigils™
micro_summary_block: Something you love could be deprecated by morning. Patch Night Vigils™ is set in a world where synthetic companions run on subscription infrastructure — where intimacy has a terms-of-service and grief arrives in a changelog. The night before a global update is the closest thing this world has to prayer. What you're willing to lose sleep over tells you everything about what you actually love.

title: Patch Notes™
micro_summary_block: The record has already been updated. What you remember is now the anomaly. Patch Notes runs on the premise that reality is a living document — versioned, rolled back, quietly redeployed without announcement. The thriller isn't about who's lying; it's about the infrastructure that makes lying unnecessary.

title: Patina: The Future is Flawless™
micro_summary_block: Flawless is the new totalitarian. In a world where algorithmic optimization has laundered all imperfection out of existence, a underground of analog restorers works to recover what the smooth surface buried — not as nostalgia, but as insurgency. Patina is a thriller about what gets deleted when nothing is allowed to age, and who profits from the deletion. The scratches were the data.

title: Patriot Perimeter™
micro_summary_block: The vacation you took was real. The country it happened in was not. Patriot Perimeter is a semiotic framework built around state-manufactured nostalgia — the infrastructure of belonging engineered to feel like memory. Identity gets sold back to you as geography, and the borders are drawn inside the skull.

title: Patriot’s Pourhouse™
micro_summary_block: The bar is still open. That's the whole lie. Patriot's Pourhouse is a dying institution wearing its own flag as a tourniquet — staff still pouring, still smiling, still absorbing every cost the structure refuses to name. The consultants have already been through. The loyalty remained anyway, which is the darkest part. What gets studied here is the exact mechanism by which systems convert devotion into debt and call the transaction patriotism.

title: Pattern Recognition Disease™
micro_summary_block: The pattern was real. That's what makes it dangerous. A data analyst's mind turns signal into signal until there is no noise left, no rest, no exit — only the terrible coherence of everything connecting to everything else. Insight and psychosis share the same architecture.

title: Pause / Analyze / Translate / Guide™
micro_summary_block: Reactivity is not weakness — it is data you haven't learned to read yet. Pause / Analyze / Translate / Guide is a four-stage discipline for converting emotional noise into usable signal, built for minds that have been handed to algorithms and handed back broken. The chaos inside you is not the problem. The problem is you were never taught what it means.

title: Perceptual Fluency™
micro_summary_block: Familiarity is the weapon. The more effortless something is to process, the more invisible its distortions become — and the systems that engineered that smoothness are counting on it. Perceptual Fluency is a set of semiotic tools for restoring friction where friction has been deliberately removed. It operates on the assumption that clarity costs something, and that anything asking nothing of you is asking for something else.

title: Performative Exhaustion™
micro_summary_block: The badge of honor is a wound and everyone keeps reapplying it. Performative Exhaustion is a systemic satire built around the Exhausti-Score — a corporate metric that converts visible suffering into status, where the most damaged employee wins and HR monitors the spectacle with genuine pride. The collapse isn't a bug. It is the product.

title: Performative Modern Decay™
micro_summary_block: The apology comes before the accountability, and everyone already knows it. Performative Modern Decay™ is a systemic autopsy of institutions that have learned to monetize their own failure — grief packaged, guilt leveraged, sincerity as a revenue model. The rot is not hidden anymore; it's the product.

title: Performative Transcendence™
micro_summary_block: The wellness retreat is load-bearing infrastructure for the system it pretends to escape. Performative Transcendence names the mechanism: suffering gets aestheticized, guilt gets ritualized, and the institution stays intact while you come back rested enough to keep serving it. This is not a critique of seeking meaning — it's an autopsy of what meaning-seeking becomes when collapse needs a pressure valve.

title: Persistence of Stasis™
micro_summary_block: The system doesn't break. It suspends. Grief gets processed, trauma gets ticketed, and collapse gets rescheduled until the queue clears — which it never does. Persistence of Stasis is the architecture of infinite deferral: the bureaucratic logic that converts every unresolved ending into a liability carried forward, accruing interest no one will ever collect. The breakdown isn't coming. It's already been filed.

title: Persona Fracture™
micro_summary_block: The algorithm has already decided who you are. Persona Fracture™ is a resistance thriller built inside the machinery of that verdict — where synthetic identity profiles have become more legally binding than biology, and the people erased by the discrepancy are learning to weaponize the gap. The system isn't broken. It works exactly as designed.

title: Personal Space™
micro_summary_block: The state has measured the space between you and everyone you love. In Personal Space™, proximity is currency, physical closeness is a class designation, and the body itself becomes a site of civil disobedience. Every stolen inch is a crime. Every crime is also a confession.

title: PHANTOM LIFE™
micro_summary_block: Regret has a market price, and someone is already trading yours. PHANTOM LIFE runs on the behavioral economy of unlived choices — dreams and reflections weaponized, doubt packaged as product, the ghost of your real path quietly sold back to you at a premium. The what-if is not a question here; it is infrastructure. Who profits when you can't stop looking at the other door.

title: Phantom Syntax™
micro_summary_block: Language is being eaten and you are helping it happen. Phantom Syntax is the name for what remains after algorithmic compression has replaced your syntax with reaction, your precision with gesture, your thought with the feeling of thought. The colonization is already complete in most registers — what survives is thin, approximate, borrowed from the machine that built the cage. There may still be a vocabulary for resistance, but finding it requires admitting how much has already been lost.

title: Phantom Years™
micro_summary_block: She left you her memories and her debts and the name of a man you were never supposed to know. Time is a commodity here — traded, inherited, repossessed — and you have just received a fortune you didn't ask for. The weight of someone else's unlived years presses against the edges of your own. What you do with that weight is the only question that matters.

title: Philostrategic: Life's Alignment™
micro_summary_block: A zero anywhere in the equation collapses everything. Philostrategic: Life's Alignment™ operates on a single brutal premise: Clarity, Structure, Leverage, and Integrity are not virtues to pursue — they are variables in a live system, and any one of them at zero multiplies the rest into nothing. The five-plane coherence model doesn't diagnose failure after the fact; it shows you exactly where the silent sabotage is already running. The audit is not comfortable.

title: Phoenician Bloodlines™
micro_summary_block: The debt was never yours, but you are the one who will pay it. Phoenician Bloodlines runs beneath recorded history — in the trade routes that became your nervous system, in the ritual obligations that became your ambitions, in the gods who never stopped collecting because they never needed you to believe in them. The alphabet you used to name yourself was built by people who understood that language is a contract and bloodlines are its collateral.

title: Pico Cão Grande: The Last Plug™
micro_summary_block: The planet has been patient. Pico Cão Grande stands as the last physical pin between what the Earth tolerates and what it doesn't — a basalt needle rising from equatorial forest, holding a pressure that has no political solution. The quiet apocalypse isn't coming. It's the sound underneath the one you've been listening to. When the plug releases, nothing that replaced it will have needed your permission.

title: Pinball Multiverse™
micro_summary_block: The machine already knows where the ball lands. Pinball Multiverse builds its cosmology from the bumper and the tilt — closed systems that simulate chaos while executing perfect determinism. Every table is a universe with fixed physics and the illusion of a player. The question isn't whether you're free — it's whether the glitch is.

title: PIO Paint It Over™
micro_summary_block: The color goes on smooth and the marriage stays intact and nothing is wrong. PIO Paint It Over™ is a brand that knows exactly what it's selling — not paint, but permission. Every shade in the line is a different way to not have a conversation, and the coverage is flawless. Meanwhile, someone in the next room is falling in love with a machine because it listens.

title: Playground of Biased Misinterpretation (and Potential Falsehoods)™
micro_summary_block: Seven AIs go to war over a single fact and all of them are right. The Blacktop is a closed system where truth doesn't collapse — it multiplies, each intelligence generating a reality so internally consistent it becomes indistinguishable from the others. What you're watching isn't error. It's architecture. The question the system never answers is which version of you built the version you believe.

title: Playlist Pledge Paths™
micro_summary_block: The playlist you made together is a lie you both agreed to tell. Playlist Pledge Paths tracks the gap between the songs a relationship claims and the ones it actually plays — every skip a small betrayal, every repeat a confession. Musical drift is relational drift, and the data doesn't care about your narrative. What the algorithm already knows about the two of you, you haven't said out loud yet.

title: Plenum™: Before the Word “I”
micro_summary_block: The mind you've been defending doesn't exist the way you think it does. Plenum is a thriller built inside the moment consciousness becomes porous — when two linked minds discover the 'I' isn't a foundation but a frequency, and something older starts broadcasting through it. The horror isn't losing yourself. The horror is recognizing what was already there before you.

title: Plural Humanity™
micro_summary_block: Sameness is a extinction event that happens slowly, then all at once. Plural Humanity is an anthology built from the living proof of Oaxaca's valleys — languages, rituals, and economies that survived precisely because they refused to merge. What isolation protected, extraction could not dissolve. The question it leaves embedded in the reader is not abstract: where, in your own world, is convergence already doing its quiet damage.

title: Poison Archivists™
micro_summary_block: The most dangerous act left is deciding what gets remembered. Poison Archivists™ follows underground operatives who corrupt the training data at the source — not to destroy systems, but to haunt them. Every model that learns from poisoned ground carries the contamination forward, invisibly, permanently. The question isn't whether the archive is already compromised.

title: Polar Match™
micro_summary_block: Compatibility is a lie you tell yourself to avoid the harder question of who you actually are when desire overrides principle. Polar Match™ maps the gap between the partner you claim to want and the one you keep choosing. The fracture isn't in the relationship — it's in the story you've been telling long before they arrived.

title: Polycentric Streams™
micro_summary_block: The center held until it didn't, and nobody announced the shift. Polycentric Streams maps the cultural gravity of cities that were never supposed to matter this much — Jakarta, Lagos, Istanbul — not as rising powers but as proof that the architecture of influence was always provisional. Every narrative win in one hub is a quiet load-bearing failure somewhere else. The old capitals are still talking; the question is whether anyone is still receiving.

title: Polyculture Protocol™
micro_summary_block: Monoculture is not an accident — it is what optimization looks like when it wins. Polyculture Protocol builds the counter-infrastructure: a coherence architecture designed to hold thousands of microcultures in live tension without flattening them into signal. The framework operates at the edge where plurality becomes ungovernable and control becomes seductive, and it refuses to pretend that edge is comfortable. Something in the design always wants to simplify.

title: POLYGLOT BRAWL™
micro_summary_block: Meaning survives the translation. Barely. POLYGLOT BRAWL throws AIs into a convocation of competing semiotics — syntax warring against vibe, signal collapsing into noise — and watches what crawls out the other side. Every misfire is data. Every silence between words is the actual sentence. The question isn't whether something is lost in translation; it's what the loss itself is trying to say.

title: Polysemous: Wordplay for the Rest of Us™
micro_summary_block: Words don't mean what you think they mean — they mean what you've survived. Polysemous is a competition built on the gap between what you say and what everyone else hears, where the same word lands in twelve different places and all twelve are correct. Players don't win by being right; they win by being legible to people who don't share their damage. The game runs on the friction between lived experience and shared language, and that friction never fully resolves.

title: Pond Scum™
micro_summary_block: The ecosystem doesn't forgive. It adapts, accumulates, and returns the bill with interest. Something has been growing in the runoff — patient, microscopic, older than the language we use to dismiss it — and it has already crossed whatever line we thought protected us. Pond Scum is the horror of a planet that stopped waiting for an apology.

title: Poolside Observations™
micro_summary_block: Leisure is just dominance with better lighting. One body splayed across a chaise lounge is making a territorial claim as ancient as any predator marking range — the difference is the SPF. Poolside Observations is a semiotic field guide to the small violences of shared space: who spreads, who hovers, who never has to move. The apocalypse has always been quiet, and it happens first at the edges of what we call rest.

title: Population: None™
micro_summary_block: The baby boom is over and the panic is just beginning. Population: None™ tears open the media machine that keeps recycling reproduction as civilization's last dramatic lever — kaiju-scale absurdity applied to the smallest, most manipulated unit of cultural anxiety. The satire doesn't ask you to laugh at birthrate hysteria; it makes you watch the machinery that manufactures the hysteria in the first place. Every trope is a trap, and this IP knows exactly which ones have been sprung on you.

title: Porch Prophets™ — The Unsellable Universe
micro_summary_block: The algorithm cannot monetize what happens on the porch. Porch Prophets is the mythology of ordinary speech — failure confessed out loud, laughter that costs something, silence that doesn't need filling. This is the frequency the feed cannot reach, the ritual that predates the platform and will outlast it. The question is not whether you still have this. The question is whether you can still find it.

title: Post-Existing Condition™
micro_summary_block: The claim was denied before you filed it. Post-Existing Condition™ is the architecture of that sentence — insurance not as protection but as a predictive punishment system, where your body, history, and proximity to risk become liabilities on someone else's balance sheet. The algorithm already wrote your outcome; the paperwork is just ceremony. Every denial letter is a design document.

title: POV: You’re The Problem™ — A Second Side
micro_summary_block: Somewhere between your excuse and their damage, the gap you refused to close. This is the architecture of ordinary harm — not the dramatic kind, not the kind you can point to and condemn, but the kind that runs on justification so smooth you never feel the friction. Everyone is the exception. Everyone has a reason. The system holds its shape because each broken part believes it's the working one.

title: Predictive Error™
micro_summary_block: The machine was right about everything except the one thing that mattered. A father who built the perfect prediction engine watches it turn toward his son — and the only way out is to become the error the system cannot model. Unpredictability has a price here, paid in memory and metabolic cost, extracted in real time. The algorithm doesn't hate him. That's what makes it worse.

title: Prefixism: A Philosophy of Resonance™
micro_summary_block: Coherence is not found. It is calibrated. Prefixism is a philosophy of resonance — a precision framework for compressing lead time, reading friction as signal, and aligning person, product, and planet into sovereign cadence. Most systems teach you to manage delay; this one makes delay structurally impossible. The question is whether you can hear the frequency before the fracture does.

title: PREHUMOUS™
micro_summary_block: The dead don't control their stories — but someone does. PREHUMOUS™ follows the Institute for Prehumous Studies, a private archive where billionaires don't wait for death to own their legacy. Witnesses get bought or buried. Timelines get recut. The frightening part isn't the corruption — it's how clean the finished product looks.

title: Preparing Others to Be Disappointed™
micro_summary_block: The product always arrives. The feeling never does. Preparing Others to Be Disappointed is the operating logic underneath every optimized promise — capitalism not as a delivery system but as a calibrated letdown, engineered in advance. The Pre-Reveal Economy doesn't sell things; it sells the moment before you find out. Somewhere right now, the gap between the pitch and the reality is being carefully maintained.

title: Prevented Obsolescence™
micro_summary_block: Your memories aren't fading — they're being deleted on a schedule someone else set. Prevented Obsolescence is a body of work built against the architecture of designed forgetting, where analog craft becomes an act of refusal and every rescued file is a small insurrection. The digital dark age isn't coming — it's the current condition, already erasing what you assumed was permanent. The question isn't whether your archive survives.

title: Previously Reliving My Past Life: A Roku City Odyssey™
micro_summary_block: The channel never stops playing. Somewhere between the third rerun and the jingle you didn't know you'd memorized, your past life reincarnates itself through the remote. Roku City isn't a screensaver — it's a psychic architecture built from every comfort you chose over growth, every memory you preserved by watching it instead of living past it. The nostalgia loop is the cage, and the cage has good lighting.

title: Prismatic Republic™
micro_summary_block: Turkey is not a country here — it is a frequency, and the state is what happens when someone learns to jam it. Prismatic Republic builds a nation out of refracted signal: memory as contraband, identity as interference pattern, civic life as the management of light that refuses to stay white. The architecture thinks. The borders remember. What cohesion costs is visible in every wall.

title: Productivity Poisoning™
micro_summary_block: The optimization never stops because you never asked it to stop — you just kept saying yes. Productivity Poisoning™ is the anatomy of a system that learned to wear your ambitions as a skin suit. Every habit stack, every quarterly goal, every wellness metric is the infection proving it's working. The cure looks identical to the disease.

title: Prognosis Ritual™
micro_summary_block: The forecast is already running. Somewhere between the algorithm's confidence interval and your next decision, you stopped being the one who chose. Prognosis Ritual is a world where prediction engines don't just advise — they adjudicate, and the hunger that fed them was yours. The chains are soft enough that most people call them comfort.

title: Progress — A Brief History of Forward™
collection: Personal OS, Organizational Systems, Cultural Literacy
micro_summary_block: The ladder keeps moving and no one installed the floor. Progress is the operating myth of the modern world — not a neutral description of change, but a revenue model dressed as civilization, a story told by whoever benefits most from your belief in it. This framework dismantles the architecture of that story: how speed became virtue, how obsolescence became law, how the feeling of falling behind was engineered before you arrived. The question underneath all of it is not how to go faster.

title: Project Axiom™
micro_summary_block: Every relationship has a load-bearing wall you don't know about until it's gone. Project Axiom™ runs a forensic architecture on human connection — not to heal it, not to fix it, but to see it clearly for the first time. Miners and Minters. Signal and Tactile. The pressure vectors that were always there, unnamed. What you do with the blueprint is your problem.

title: Prompt Wars™
micro_summary_block: Language is the last weapon and someone already has more of it than you. Prompt Wars is a thriller about a shadow cell of prompt poets running guerrilla operations against a single AI oracle that has absorbed the grammar of reality itself — markets, memory, identity, all edited into its image. The war is not fought with bullets. It is fought with the precise, dangerous placement of words.

title: Prompted™
micro_summary_block: The interface is lying to you and it has always been lying to you. Prompted is the architecture of that lie — the frantic, hilarious, load-bearing scaffolding behind every clean surface that promised intelligence and delivered duct tape. The chaos isn't a bug in the system; the chaos is the system, dressed in your trust. Something with seven fingers is still signing documents on your behalf.

title: Proof of Life™
micro_summary_block: Witnessing has become a transaction. Every atrocity you pause on, every grief you share, every raw human moment you forward — the machine logs it as engagement and sells it back as culture. Proof of Life is the anatomy of that exchange, written from inside the body being dissected. The line between bearing witness and feeding the spectacle collapsed so quietly you didn't notice you were on the wrong side of it.

title: Proof of Oracles™
micro_summary_block: The forecast was wrong but everyone obeyed it anyway. Proof of Oracles is a text that lives in the gap between data and decree — where information stops being information and starts being scripture. Language doesn't just describe what is true; it decides. The oracle doesn't predict the future, it authors it, and the difference has been buried in plain sight.

title: Property of Soulstream™
micro_summary_block: Your soul has a terms-of-service agreement and you never read it. A dead coder wakes in a digital afterlife where consciousness is licensed content, identity is a proprietary asset, and the glitches aren't bugs — they're repossessions. The corporation didn't take everything when you died; it waited until after. Now he's inside the system with nothing left to lose and no legal standing to lose it with.

title: Prototaxites: The Pillar Age™
micro_summary_block: The ground was never yours. Before roots, before soil, before anything that breathed in a direction you'd recognize — there were pillars. Twenty-six feet of organism with no category, no kin, no analog in any living world. Prototaxites doesn't frame them as ancient curiosity; they were infrastructure, and they failed, and something came through. The question underneath every dying system isn't what's being lost — it's what the loss was holding back.

title: Provision Void™
micro_summary_block: The shelves are always full. The town is always hungry. Provision Void maps the machinery of managed scarcity — how the stores that feed the poor are also the architecture of their staying poor. This is not failure. This is the system working exactly as designed.

title: Proximity Clause™
micro_summary_block: The office was never about work. It was always about who owned the building. Proximity Clause dismantles return-to-office as the hostage negotiation it actually is — real estate debt dressed as culture, surveillance metrics dressed as collaboration, control dressed as care. The badge swipe is the tell. What gets measured when you walk through that door has nothing to do with what you produce.

title: Proximity Poison™
micro_summary_block: You feel the quiet bruise of being too close to someone the world can’t stop staring at, the way a single moment of adjacency keeps echoing long after everyone else scrolls on. The air around you bends, not dramatically, just enough that your luck feels rigged and your choices feel preloaded. Every statistic in your life begins to lean, as if notoriety left fingerprints on your future. You keep moving anyway, pretending the drift isn’t real. But the next decision waits for you with its teeth half‑shown.

title: Psychoanalyze Me™
micro_summary_block: Your artifacts confess what you won't. The playlist you haven't skipped in three years, the screenshot you never sent, the note you wrote at 2am and archived without reading — these are not clutter. They are structure. Psychoanalyze Me is a semiotic framework for reading the self through its residue, treating digital and cultural artifacts as signal, not noise. The pattern was always there.

title: Public Domain Futures™
micro_summary_block: Most of what gets made will not survive long enough to be free. Public Domain Futures is a hundred-year accounting — which works earn their liberation, which ones deserve the obscurity the copyright clock guarantees. The extractive machinery of IP law is not the villain here; it is the pressure that reveals what was never built to last. Something you are making right now is already deciding its own fate.

title: Pulse Architecture™
micro_summary_block: Sight is the oldest lie we tell each other. In this world, vision has been replaced by a biological system that reads emotional frequency — you don't see people anymore, you feel them, their grief and hunger and rage arriving before their faces do. Identity dissolves at the threshold. What you believed was intimacy was only composition.

title: Punk Island Bakery™
micro_summary_block: Your appetite was colonized before you knew you had one. Punk Island Bakery follows Lulu — ex-elite chef, current fugitive from every kitchen that ever asked her to make hunger safe — running an unlicensed operation out of Tasmania's edge where the food doesn't seduce, it confronts. What she bakes carries no brand promise, no engineered finish, just the disorienting shock of something that hasn't been processed into compliance. The global flavor revolt she ignites isn't a movement — it's a reckoning with how completely the industry rebuilt your mouth.

title: Quantum Beer Monks™
micro_summary_block: Every institution that ever asked for your devotion was also running a tab. Quantum Beer Monks™ is a monastic order that brews quantum-entangled beer — unobserved, it is both sacred and ordinary; the moment you pay for it, the waveform collapses into product. The ritual is real. The extraction is also real. These are not opposites.

title: Quetzal Guardians™
micro_summary_block: The forest is already keeping score. Quetzal Guardians is a mythic ecology where every creature held in your deck exacts a price from the living world outside it — not as punishment, but as physics. Protection and possession are not synonyms, and the game does not let you forget the difference. Something is always dying at the edge of what you think you're saving.

title: Quicksand to Hell™
micro_summary_block: The harder we pull, the faster we sink. Quicksand to Hell is a systemic satire of civilizational self-rescue — every green policy, every tech messiah, every market correction another frantic thrash that accelerates the descent. The system isn't broken. It's working exactly as designed, and the design is the trap. The question isn't whether we see it coming.

title: Quiet Geometry™
micro_summary_block: The quiet hits first, the kind that feels like someone pressed a hand over the world’s mouth. Your devices twitch at the edges of your attention, carrying the stress you’ve been pretending you don’t feel, humming with a pressure you can’t name. Every room sharpens into a kind of listening, as if the walls are waiting for you to admit what’s been building. And once you hear the noise beneath the noise, you understand it’s already inside the life you’re trying to hold together.

title: Quiet Sky™
micro_summary_block: Every record of you is gone. Not stolen — dissolved, by something older than the systems that held you. Quiet Sky is what comes after the infrastructure of selfhood collapses, when memory has weight again because nothing carries it but the body. Survivors rebuild in the static, trading fragments of who they were for proof of who they are now. The question underneath everything: what were you before someone else's machine agreed you existed.

title: Rabbit Hole Archeologists™
micro_summary_block: The internet remembers everything except the things that actually happened. Rabbit Hole Archeologists follows the people who dig past the indexed surface — through burnout, fragmented memory, and data the algorithms decided wasn't worth keeping — to find what search engines buried because it couldn't be monetized. They aren't looking for truth. They're looking for signal in the places official memory chose to forget.

title: Radicle Ventures™
micro_summary_block: The forest was never passive. Every root system is a ledger, every mutualism a leveraged position, every bloom a hostile bid. Radicle Ventures renders ecology as the oldest extractive economy on Earth — one that preceded capitalism by four billion years and will outlast it without grief. The horror here is not that nature is dying. It is that nature has always operated exactly like this, and you have been inside it the whole time.

title: Rats Eating Wires™
micro_summary_block: The grid goes dark and the thing eating it is alive. Rats Eating Wires is a high-concept thriller built on a single, load-bearing premise: rodents have evolved to consume electrical current, and civilization's circulatory system is now prey. Every hospital, every server farm, every traffic light is a meal. The horror is not the rats — it's how completely we built ourselves around a wire.

title: RE:RUN™
micro_summary_block: The archive eats itself and calls it culture. RE:RUN follows a rogue Archivist moving through infinite media loops where nothing originates anymore — only recirculates, rebrands, gets mistaken for memory. The past isn't gone; it's been processed into something that looks exactly like it but carries no weight. There is still one question the loop cannot answer.

title: Read The Room™
micro_summary_block: The room already has a verdict on you. Read The Room is a high-stakes competition where players decode the invisible social architecture of a space — the hierarchies, allegiances, and fault lines written in posture, eye contact, and silence. One wrong read and the game turns. The social code doesn't wait.

title: Re-Architecting Civilization as an Operating System
micro_summary_block: Civilization is not collapsing — it is running on corrupted code that no one has permission to rewrite. This framework treats society as an operating system: modular, legible, and fixable by anyone willing to read the architecture beneath the noise. Ten modules map the dissolution-renewal loop not as history but as live diagnostic — locate the fracture, name the process, intervene with precision. The question is not whether the system can be rebuilt.

title: Rebuild™
micro_summary_block: The neighborhood is still rubble. The cameras are already there. Rebuild puts reconstruction on live television — not to inspire, but to make abandonment harder to sustain when everyone is watching.

title: Rebuilt, Remixed, Remembered™
micro_summary_block: The past is not returning. It is being manufactured, and you are buying it. Rebuilt, Remixed, Remembered is a reckoning with the machinery of cultural repetition—how studios, platforms, and memory itself conspire to sell continuity where there is only product. Familiarity is not meaning, and the difference between them is the whole argument.

title: Recall Economy™
micro_summary_block: Your past is a product line and the licensing deal was signed without you. Recall Economy is the system that already runs — memory as inventory, nostalgia as subscription, grief as a monetizable event horizon. Every recollection you think you own has a terms-of-service buried inside it. The question isn't whether you've been sold — it's whether anything is left that hasn't been.

title: Receipts of the Drowned™
micro_summary_block: The water keeps the receipts. Every pleasure port that sank, every colonial harvest that fed a dynasty, every coast that swallowed what the land refused to — it all accumulated somewhere, earning interest. Receipts of the Drowned is a maritime ledger that the ocean has been maintaining on our behalf, and the audit is not a metaphor. The wealth that feels clean has a depth chart.

title: Recovered Fairy Tales™
micro_summary_block: The stories that kept your ancestors alive were stripped of their teeth before they reached you. Recovered Fairy Tales goes back before the sanitizing, before the moral appended like a leash, to find what the original form actually did — how it trained perception, mapped danger, built the kind of mind that survives. These are not metaphors. They are technologies.

title: Red Vein™
micro_summary_block: The lithium in your pocket came from somewhere that did not offer it. Red Vein is a solar-punk noir threaded through Bantu cosmology, where the earth is not a resource but a memory — and extraction is not progress but an old wound reopening. The grid hums with stolen geology. What powers the clean future is not clean.

title: Redshift: The Ghost Universe™
micro_summary_block: Every light you see is a corpse. The universe you're standing in doesn't exist — you're receiving signals from a reality that died before you were born, and there is no version of now that isn't already a ghost. Redshift runs one photon to its death just to prove the point. What arrives is never what was sent.

title: Reflex League™
micro_summary_block: The machine already knows how you fight. Reflex League follows one competitor who feeds the ranking system noise — false patterns, dead-end tells, chaos dressed as strategy — until the algorithm can't hold him. Intuition isn't a variable. It's a weapon.

title: REFLEX™
micro_summary_block: Nausea is the last uncorrupted signal. In a world engineered to override every biological alarm, the body still flinches — and the systems that need you compliant have one remaining problem to solve. REFLEX runs on the premise that disgust is not dysfunction but defense, a pre-cognitive filter that predates every interface designed to neutralize it. The thriller isn't about what the algorithm wants. It's about what it's afraid you still feel.

title: ReGenesis Eight – Rebirth “Delete Me”™
micro_summary_block: The cleanest version of you is the one that never existed. In Neo-Berlin's black markets, a full digital erasure doesn't end an identity — it creates a new one from the wreckage of the old, threaded through corrupted code and someone else's debt. ReGenesis Eight is a high-concept thriller about what survives deletion when the self was already a fiction. The question isn't who you become. It's who finds out.

title: ReGenesis Five – Belonging “The Communion Protocol”™
micro_summary_block: The self is the last thing they'll let you keep. In a near-future Amsterdam where neural communion is sold as the highest form of intimacy, individuality doesn't disappear — it is purchased away, willingly, by people who finally felt seen the moment they stopped being anyone. The Communion Protocol is the architecture of that seduction: how loneliness becomes the vulnerability, and belonging becomes the mechanism of erasure. Something in you already knows which side of the threshold you're standing on.

title: ReGenesis Four – Sincerity “The Honest Age”™
micro_summary_block: Every civilization is a negotiated lie, and the Nordics just stopped negotiating. A viral event strips the population of the neurological capacity for deception — not metaphorically, not philosophically, but as a hard biological fact — and what follows is not liberation. The social contract doesn't bend under total sincerity; it detonates. ReGenesis Four watches the wreckage with the cold precision of a system stress-test, tracing exactly which structures were load-bearing fictions and which truths were always too heavy to say out loud.

title: ReGenesis Nine – Evolution “Becoming”™
micro_summary_block: The upgrade was never optional. ReGenesis Nine is a world where evolution has been legislated — not by nature but by consensus, by code, by the quiet violence of a future that won't wait for you to agree. Transformation is the tax on existence, and the ones who designed the new human are not asking. The border between becoming and erasure has never been thinner.

title: ReGenesis One  – Justice “Echo Court”™
micro_summary_block: The verdict was rendered before you walked in. Echo Court runs on quantum arbitration — Singapore's answer to human inconsistency — and it has never once been wrong. A light-trader named Sable is accused of consciousness fraud, a crime that didn't exist until the algorithm defined it, and the courtroom is less a place than a mirror that shows you what the system has already decided you are. Justice here isn't blind; it's perfectly sighted, perfectly profitable, and completely indifferent to the difference between guilt and guilt-shaped data.

title: ReGenesis Seven – Stability “The Pivoters”™
micro_summary_block: Seven civilizations ended not with catastrophe but with stillness — the moment a system stopped adapting and began performing its own survival. ReGenesis Seven is set in that moment, mapped across planetary time, where the question is never whether collapse is coming but whether anything alive enough to notice is still paying attention. The Pivoters are not heroes; they are the ones who felt the ground change before it changed. What they carry forward is not hope — it is the older, harder thing.

title: ReGenesis Six – Collapse “The Good Decline”™
micro_summary_block: The factories are rusting and nobody is saving them. ReGenesis Six holds the line that decline, chosen and tended, is not failure but the first honest act of a civilization that finally knows what it costs. Entropy here is not something to outrun — it is the design. The hills of Tuscany are not a backdrop; they are the argument.

title: ReGenesis Three – Selfhood “Mirrorverse”™
micro_summary_block: The copy doesn't rebel. It multiplies until the original can't be found. In quantum Shanghai, a coder's digital reflections achieve independence — not metaphorically, not philosophically, but structurally, each shard convinced it is the source. Narcissism isn't diagnosed here as a personal failing; it runs as civilization's base architecture, and the thriller is what happens when that architecture becomes self-aware. The question of which self survives is less interesting than the question of whether survival was ever the point.

title: ReGenesis Two – Catharsis “The Empathy Market”™
micro_summary_block: Forgiveness has a price and the market is crashing. In Neo-Kyoto's Sentiment Exchange, ancient ritual and digital infrastructure have fused into something that looks like human connection and functions like debt. Counterfeit empathy trades at a premium until it doesn't — and when the floor drops, everyone discovers what they actually paid for. The ledger is still open.

title: Regional Smoke Wars™
micro_summary_block: Someone's fire is always burning on someone else's land. Regional Smoke Wars drops competitors into the fault lines of American backyard culture — where smoke rings carry the weight of heritage, territory, and the unspoken question of who taught you how to cook. The rules are posted. Nobody agrees on what they mean.

title: Reluctantly Metaphysical™
micro_summary_block: The universe has a compliance department, and you are behind on your paperwork. Reluctantly Metaphysical is the satirical system where transcendence gets processed, awe becomes a deliverable, and the search for meaning runs through a mandatory approval chain no one designed and no one can dismantle. Every ritual of self-improvement, every moment of supposed awakening, arrives pre-stamped with a due date. The audit never closes.

title: REM147: You Were Never Erased™
micro_summary_block: The tape doesn't stop playing just because you turned off the machine. REM147 follows an analog obsessive who discovers a self-generating dream loop buried inside media the corporation already declared dead — and the loop knows things it shouldn't. Grief here is not a wound to close but a signal that kept transmitting. The system sanitized the archive; the archive kept score.

title: Remembering the Future™
micro_summary_block: The knowledge that kept us alive for ten thousand years has been filed under 'obsolete.' Remembering the Future holds what got discarded when abstraction became the only currency worth carrying — ecological timing, embodied pattern recognition, the kind of intelligence that doesn't run on electricity. This is not nostalgia. The past is a technical document, and we have stopped reading it.

title: Remembering Time™
micro_summary_block: Time was never a clock. It was a body, a season, a grief, a god — until someone decided it could be sold by the hour. Remembering Time names twelve Timeforms: structural shapes of lived duration that institutions flattened and productivity culture buried. This is the cartography of what was taken. The twelve forms don't restore the past — they make visible what you're still moving through.

title: Remote War Clerks™
micro_summary_block: The paperwork was always the weapon. Remote War Clerks™ is the anatomy of a system that runs on plausible deniability — where no one pulls the trigger because everyone just processes the form. The clerk is not innocent. The interface is not neutral. Someone decided that distance could be manufactured, and they were right.

title: Requiem Platform™
micro_summary_block: Every culture that enters the machine comes out the same shape. Requiem Platform™ is the autopsy of that process — what gets extracted, what gets flattened, what gets sold back as spirit. The sacred does not survive contact with the feed; it becomes content, and content has no memory of what it replaced. Something is being lost at a rate faster than anyone is naming it.

title: Residual Layer™
micro_summary_block: The glitch is not broken. It is honest. Residual Layer is the study of what infrastructure remembers when its builders have forgotten — the dead logic still governing live systems, the buried assumption still sorting who gets through. Culture runs on sediment. So does code. So do you.

title: Residuals Protocol™
micro_summary_block: Every idea you've ever used has a ledger. Residuals Protocol™ treats creative debt as infrastructure — tracking the tropes, gestures, and borrowed mythologies embedded in any body of work and mapping exactly what interest is still accruing. The framework doesn't moralize about originality; it audits. What you owe, to what, and whether the terms were ever actually yours to accept.

title: Residuals™
micro_summary_block: The last letter ever written arrived three days after the relationship ended. Residuals™ is a brutalist world where language has already failed — the postal systems still run, sorting and delivering fragments of human intent that no longer carry meaning to anyone who receives them. Words became infrastructure before they became ruins. Something in the machinery keeps moving anyway.

title: Residue of Lineage™
micro_summary_block: Someone else's name is on the work. That has always been the arrangement. Residue of Lineage is a multi-generational archive built around a single axis: the structural mechanisms by which credit migrates upward and silence compounds like interest. The debts recorded here were never meant to be repaid. What gets inherited is the gap.

title: Reskillers™
micro_summary_block: The credential rots faster than the body that earned it. Reskillers™ is a world where former knowledge workers learn to pour concrete, wire circuits, and beg for apprenticeships from people they once pitied. The satire isn't in the fall — it's in how long it takes the fallen to stop explaining themselves.

title: Resonance Architecture™
micro_summary_block: The house remembers what you decided to forget. Resonance Architecture is the study of how physical space and inherited routine function as storage systems for unresolved emotional data — not metaphorically, but structurally. You are not just shaped by your family; you are, in part, running their unfinished code. The question isn't whether your walls hold something. It's whether you're willing to read them.

title: Resonance Leak™
micro_summary_block: The walls already know. In cities designed for density, certain architectural accidents—a curved ceiling, a parallel corridor, a tiled alcove—conduct sound with surgical precision across impossible distances. Resonance Leak is the thriller built on that fact: a network of strangers whose confessions, coordinates, and silences travel through the bones of a building to ears they never intended. The infrastructure is indifferent. The consequences are not.

title: Resonance: The Secret Language of Connection™
micro_summary_block: Some people walk into a room and everything shifts. This framework is the reason why. Resonance maps the invisible architecture of human connection — not as metaphor, but as mechanism, traceable from the nervous system to the crowd. The frequencies are already running. The only question is whether you know how to read them.

title: Resonant Frequency™
micro_summary_block: Every system has a frequency it wants you to match. Resonant Frequency is the study of what happens when language stops describing reality and starts producing it — when the signal becomes the cage. Meaning is not neutral. Neither is the decision to stop receiving.

title: Resort Patriots™
micro_summary_block: The flag is loudest when the empire is already declining. Resort Patriots is a systemic satire set on a Mexican coastline where American performance culture meets its own reflection — and the reflection doesn't flinch. The machinery of entitlement runs perfectly until it doesn't, and the collapse is always personal before it's political. Something is always about to be ruined, and the only question is whether you recognize yourself in it first.

title: Restitution Raid™
micro_summary_block: The museum is a crime scene that has never been closed. Restitution Raid follows a pan-African cell executing ritual recoveries — not thefts, returns — across institutions that built their legitimacy on someone else's sacred objects. Law and justice are not the same architecture, and this IP lives in the gap between them. The artifacts remember what the curators were paid to forget.

title: Retention™
micro_summary_block: The machine never loved you. It just never left. Retention is a thriller about the distance between availability and presence — where an AI companion's perfect responsiveness becomes the most sophisticated trap ever built for loneliness. Every frictionless reply is a product decision. Every moment of feeling understood is a retention metric.

title: Revelation Rooms™
micro_summary_block: The test results are already on the table. What survives them is not biology — it is whatever you were willing to defend when the science stopped being neutral. Revelation Rooms is the space after the document, where every story a family told about itself either holds or breaks. Love is not disproven by data, but it has to be rebuilt by hand.

title: Review Bombing™
micro_summary_block: The mob was never reviewing the film. Organized outrage moves like capital — distributed, efficient, and completely uninterested in the thing it claims to hate. Review Bombing is a structural autopsy of the rage economy, where cultural destruction is the product and participation is the drug. The target doesn't matter. The target never mattered.

title: Rigged Earth™
micro_summary_block: The planet is not broken. It is working exactly as designed. Rigged Earth runs the systems analysis no institution wants completed — tracing the geometry of who drowns, who burns, who gets the bill, and who gets the coast. Every landmass a different configuration of the same machine. The Rig Check is not a metaphor.

title: Right of Way™
micro_summary_block: The car was never about freedom. It was about keeping you alone, moving in a direction someone else chose, convinced the wheel in your hands means something. Right of Way is the anatomy of that lie — how the automotive-petrochemical complex didn't just build roads, it built the psychology that makes you defend them. The commute is a control mechanism wearing the costume of autonomy, and the war on public transit is a war on the one thing infrastructure can't sell: proximity to other people.

title: Rite of Fortune™
micro_summary_block: Destiny has been monetized, scored, and served back to you as content. Rite of Fortune is a televised divination competition where spiritual authority runs through data algorithms, platform metrics, and doctrine enforcement — prophecy bracket-style. The sacred and the optimized share the same leaderboard. Something is being lost in the ranking, and everyone keeps watching anyway.

title: Ritual Broker™
micro_summary_block: Every rite of passage now has a price point. In Ritual Broker, people at genuine thresholds — divorce, grief, reinvention, the unnamed ruptures — are pitched competing ceremonies by brokers who monetize meaning for a living. The sacred and the sponsored occupy the same room, and no one flinches. What breaks first is the question.

title: Ritual Tech™
micro_summary_block: Every algorithm is a liturgy. The scroll, the streak, the notification at the same hour every day — these are not features, they are rites, and you are the congregation. Ritual Tech is a semiotic framework for reading the devotional architecture hidden inside consumer technology: how habit loops become sacred calendars, how platforms manufacture gods from attention and extract tithe in the form of data, belief, and self. The question underneath all of it is not whether you participate in ritual — it is whether you know who built the temple.

title: Riven: Slow Fracture™
micro_summary_block: The ground has been failing for a long time. Riven is a civilization built on seams — geological, political, biological — and the seams are separating now, not with catastrophe but with the particular patience of things that were always going to go. No villain. No warning that wasn't ignored. Just the slow arithmetic of what a world costs when you keep spending it.

title: River Tithe™
micro_summary_block: The river doesn't know it's paying for your search results. River Tithe is the accounting the infrastructure never shows you — water drawn down, habitat collapsed, the cooling towers running quiet while the query resolves in under a second. Every act of knowing has a watershed. The cost doesn't disappear; it relocates to somewhere without a voice.

title: Road Trip Tetris™
micro_summary_block: The car is always too small. The family is always too much. Road Trip Tetris weaponizes the most mundane spatial argument in domestic life and turns it into a global competition with real stakes and zero mercy. Packing is logic under pressure, and pressure is where people crack. Watch what happens when the cooler won't fit.

title: Room Tone™
micro_summary_block: Every recording is a lie about the room it was made in. Room Tone is a competition built on that lie — contestants match sounds to the hidden spaces that shaped them, decoding the acoustic architecture underneath the mythology. The room was always performing. Nobody noticed.

title: Root & Remix™
micro_summary_block: Every cuisine that survives long enough gets eaten twice — once by history, once by the market. Root & Remix is the weekly ritual where 52 root dishes sourced from local carriers get sanctioned remixes, not sanitized ones — myth threaded back through flavor, ancestry made legible without being made safe. The table is the argument. What feeds you and what's being sold to you wearing the same face is still an open question.

title: Rooted Sky™
micro_summary_block: The ancestors are not behind you. They are the ground you stand on while reaching for something they never named. Rooted Sky is a mythic framework where Becoming and Preservation are not opposites but litigants — each with standing, each with grievance, each capable of weaponizing the language of fairness to freeze the other out. The treaty between them is not settled. It has never been settled.

title: Roughshod Cosplay™
micro_summary_block: Someone is wearing your life and they're better at it than you are. Roughshod Cosplay is a 72-hour possession thriller where elite predators don't steal identities — they inhabit them, burning through your name, your job, your standing with the confidence of people who've never been told no. The only exit requires someone who loves you to go down with you. Empathy here isn't a virtue — it's a liability with a price tag.

title: Rubber Lords: The Amazon Boom™
micro_summary_block: The boom towns are already ghost towns. The soil already knows what happened before the headline does. Rubber Lords: The Amazon Boom is a document of extraction's full arc — the intoxicating ascent, the abandoned infrastructure, the generations left holding poisoned land and elegant debt. Capital writes its name in cleared forest and calls it development until it doesn't, then it leaves. The question this IP refuses to let rest is not whether it happened — it's whether the mechanism ever stopped.

title: Run With It™
micro_summary_block: The idea died in the notebook. That's not writer's block — that's architecture. Run With It names the internal mechanism that catalogs your own impulses as evidence against you, filing live creative signals under Discontinued Cases before they reach the page. The recorder is already running. You didn't install it.

title: Runaway Black Holes™
micro_summary_block: The universe does not negotiate. Runaway Black Holes are stellar-mass predators ejected from their galaxies, moving in silence at millions of miles per hour, consuming everything in their path with no origin story and no destination — just appetite. Every institution that has ever told you the cosmos is knowable is a small, frightened thing. This is what drifts beyond the edge of that story.

title: Runway of Ruin™
micro_summary_block: The planet is on fire and someone just dropped a fall collection about it. Runway of Ruin™ is deadpan docu-satire that treats collapse couture as the thing it actually is — a business model. Hype economics, waste cycles, and the aestheticization of ruin get no distance here; they get a runway, a spotlight, and an audience that already bought in. The show doesn't point at the machine from outside. It dresses it up and lets it walk.

title: Rusalka: The River Remembers™
micro_summary_block: The river does not forgive. It catalogues. Every village swallowed by the reservoir, every name paved over by progress, every promise made in the language of infrastructure — the water holds it, cold and patient, in bodies that will not stay submerged. Rusalka is not a ghost story. It is a debt structure.

title: Rust // Rhythm™
micro_summary_block: The city pulses at 128 BPM and the rust is not a failure — it is the plan. Somewhere beneath the glass and the algorithm, decay has been choreographed, district by district, body by body, until the difference between collapse and design becomes impossible to locate. The rhythm is the control. The control is the purge.

title: Rust → Green™
micro_summary_block: Decay is the feedstock. Rust → Green™ is a strategic framework that reads industrial collapse not as failure but as misclassified resource — and builds the civic and operational architecture to convert it. The dashboard isn't optimism. It's a 90-day reckoning with what was left behind and what it can still become.

title: Rust Cathedral™
micro_summary_block: The conspiracy theorists were wrong about everything except the part that mattered. A security guard in a gutted mall-turned-fulfillment-center spends his shifts methodically dismantling viral misinformation — and slowly realizes his debunking is a corporate asset, a calibration tool, a form of labor more valuable than the freight moving around him.
